programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine with top-level security clearance, whose body has been discovered in a Beverly Hills car park - minus a hand. As inquiries proceed, the case proves to have unexpected ramifications, including the theft of jewels from Iraqi museums

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Third of an eight-part story. Diane and Nate investigate an attack on a doctor on the Jasmine Allen estate and discover his colleague Dr Rachel Cartwright (Josette Simon) has been performing illegal operations. Elsewhere, Carly Samuels' grieving mother Leanne is upset by a psychic's claim that she 'saw' Tito Morientes get into the car used in the drive-by shooting, but nobody is brave enough to give evidence leading to a prosecution

programme.desc: Jac is promoted to the position of Acting Consultant on the Keller wing, but when she clashes with Ric over a difficult patient, she loses face with her team. Guest starring Bad Girls' Victoria Alcock (Julie Saunders)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Fourth of an eight-part story. Carly's funeral descends into chaos as the crowd chant 'murderer' at chief suspect Tito Morientes and a gunshot shatters the glass of the hearse. programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: British sitcom about the misadventures of a cockney painter and decorator. Jacko finds himself in a desperate predicament - but can he get out of trouble?

programme.desc: Compo has an interesting experience when he loses his ferret as the trio make their way to a friend's funeral

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of an Asian man's body sparks another case for Lynley and Havers, plunging them into a murky world of illicit sexuality, racial tension and family loyalties. As the investigation becomes increasingly tangled and complex, Havers takes drastic action to save a child's life - which leaves her career in jeopardy. Detective drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small, with Saeed Jaffrey

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of pornographer Jim Hockney is reopened when his daughter Sara provides DNA proof he wasn't her father. The team tries to track down jazz singer Angela Gold, who was the victim's girlfriend at the time and disappeared from the music scene after being acquitted of the crime, and it's Brian's replacement Danny Griffin (Nicholas Lyndhurst) who manages to find her. However, with Angela professing her innocence and Sara's mother Jane also arousing suspicion, it becomes a struggle to determine who is telling the truth. With a guest appearance by former Corrie killer Brian Capron

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki sees a barefoot girl being knocked over by a car, marking the beginning of a case that takes the team into the harrowing world of sex grooming and teenage prostitution. The police know the girl has been abused and suspicion soon falls on her stepfather. Meanwhile, an early-morning bath saves Harry's life, and two puzzling corpses are brought in to the centre - a heavily tattooed man and a decomposed body found at the airport. Guest starring Sanjeev Bhaskar, Elyes Gabel and Madeline Duggan

programme.desc: Hugh clashes with Penny's new boyfriend, Meryl plays dirty to win the re-count of the Mayoral election, and Ajax does work experience in the hospital

programme.desc: A house guest becomes the focus of Alice's schemes. When Mary falls gravely ill, Foster's attempts to care for her antagonise the new chief. Charlotte tries to contain the smallpox epidemic and Samuel has to make a difficult decision.

programme.desc: A case of foot and mouth brings tragedy to the Dales, leaving Siegfried in quarantine and James and Tristan having to deal with all of the rounds. programme.desc: Sandra and the team investigate why five years earlier a man seemingly vanished during his morning run and has not been seen since. Tito Morientes is arrested on the testimony of Alisha Danniels for shooting Carly Samuels, but with Dwayne Fox still on the run the Sun Hill team face the problem of protecting their key witness. Fox's boss Marco Oliver is shocked at the killing and volunteers to help set up a sting operation to catch the second suspect before the rival gangs can get to Alisha

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Annalese asks Ric to move in with her while he undergoes chemotherapy, causing Michael to make a potentially career-ending move that places him in Connie's line of fire. Meanwhile, Frieda encounters an old friend while working the night shift on her 30th birthday, and Joseph tries to assert his authority by undermining Greg, but is forced to eat humble pie when he has to call for assistance in the operating theatre

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Guppy is horrified when Abs comes clean to him about witnessing the attack. A badly beaten Jas is brought to the hospital, a victim of Lee and the gang, and Harry is urged to operate. Bruno takes a backhander from an undertaker for recommending the firm to grieving relatives, then goes on to give an impassioned speech about patient care over bureaucracy. Medical drama, starring Elyes Gabel

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part six of an eight-part story. The hit-and-run attack leaves Alisha Danniels too shaken and terrified to testify against Morientes and Fox, but she does identify the third man involved in Carly's death. Emma and Nate trace the vehicle and score a lucky bonus in identifying an ammunition seller who gives them the names of all the gun dealers on the Jasmine Allen estate

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: David's affair continues to have repercussions around the Square. Kathy and Phil visit Ian in hospital. Cindy has been spotted leaving for Paris

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Terry confronts Tiffany who refuses to name the father of her baby. Pat confronts David about Cindy. April's old flame Nikos asks her to come back to Athens with him

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The soldiers help to rescue hostages in the African state of Mokindi. Meanwhile, Sadie and Paul are reunited. Starring Robson Green and Duncan Bell

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James performs an unfamiliar operation on a pedigree bull and helps his partners treat Mrs Bond's cats. Yorkshire veterinary drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer returns from six months in Spain to find a guest of Lady Jane has fled Peru with a priceless Inca ring. Joanna Lumley guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko and Eric are hired to decorate a mobile home, but end up painting the wrong one. Elmo remains blind to the fact that Paulette is only interested in him for his money

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Humphries is accused of dipping his hand in the till. His distinguished career appears to end in disgrace when he is dismissed after being found guilty. Comedy, with John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Nora Batty is seen without her wrinkled stockings on, fuelling rumours that Compo might have a rival for her affections. When, she is later observed in the pub with another man, local tongues start wagging in earnest. Rural comedy, starring Bill Owen and Kathy Staff

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg and his friends try to draw Gough away from his wife of 50 years for a bit of fun. Rural comedy, with Peter Sallis, Bill Owen, Michael Aldridge and Cardew Robinson

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Dalziel witnesses a shooting through a neighbour's window and rushes to the scene to find a dead woman, whose husband is suspected of being responsible for the incident. Feature-length drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan, with Michael Kitchen and Josette Simon

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and her seasoned colleagues reopen the case of a political aide's murder when a dormant offshore bank account containing £40,000 is discovered in his name. Ben Ransley was killed in 1997 on the evening of Labour's landslide victory and in his wallet was a photo of a beautiful blonde. But neither his mother nor his best friend was able to identify the mystery woman. Is she the key to the crime? Drama, with former Brookside star John McArdle (Billy Corkhill), Jason Durr of Heartbeat fame and one-time Bad Girls actress Kika Mirylees joining Denis Lawson, Dennis Waterman and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry is disturbed to be called to a crime scene at the home of a family he knows, and finds the bodies of the mother and her teenage daughter, while her stepson lies in a coma - although her toddler has been left unharmed. The police suspect the woman's husband to be the killer, but the pathologist is not so sure of his guilt

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon asks Kathy how Gavin died and is shocked when she realises Kathy is planning to go to the memorial service - as far as she is concerned, he may have been her biological father but he can rot in hell. Peter finds himself caught in the middle when Ash and Suki argue, and Bailey finds a stray dog - but Karen says they cannot afford to keep it

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki tells Ash she should do anything necessary to get her job back, so Peter assures her he will say he gave himself the medication. Mitch explains that Bailey is heartbroken over losing Bronson so Rainie enlists Stuart's help to find the stray dog. As an emotional Sharon packs up her things up in the Vic, Kim tries to organise her a leaving party

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira tells Hanssen that he may not have been Reyhan's only victim, leading the great surgeon to become distracted while operating on her father. Chloe realises Ange is missing and grows increasingly suspicious that Cameron has something to do with her disappearance. Louis gets into an altercation with a former friend who has turned up seeking revenge

programme.title: Roman Kemp: Our Silent Emergency
programme.desc: Last August, Roman Kemp's life changed dramatically with the sudden and unexpected death of his best friend, radio producer Joe Lyons. It led him to question why increasing numbers of young men are taking their own lives and why so many of them never ask for help. Here, Roman sets out to explore what can be done to encourage people to seek help, what preventative action others need to be taking, and the lasting impact of mental health and suicide on friends and family

programme.title: Murray Walker: A Life in the Fast Lane
programme.desc: A profile of the motor-racing commentator whose voice became one of the most recognisable on British television. The programme follows Murray as he travels to the 2011 Australian Grand Prix, looks back at highlights from his career on radio and TV and recalls his time in the Army. Featuring contributions by Nigel Mansell, Damon Hill, Michael Schumacher, Jenson Button and Lewis Hamilton

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Sheriff Romero responds to Norma's 911 call and has a surprising proposal for her concerning the deaths of Keith Summers and Zack Shelby. Even with those recent events now behind them, Norma reacts badly to Dylan's decision to move into his own place. The hotel is not quite open yet but gets its first guest Jake Abernathy, who claims to have been a regular when Summers owned the place

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is upset after Emma tells some of the other girls that he and Bradley slept together. He leaves school without permission, and in a meeting with the principal and his teacher it is suggested to Norma that Norman should seek professional help. The session with the psychologist does not go well as the patient shows signs of severe strain

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain hurdling great Colin Jackson, former England striker Darren Bent, ex-WBA Super Middleweight champion boxer George Groves and Commonwealth Games gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

Away from the house search, Ginny visits the Royal Air Force Museum Cosford and meets the conservation team reconstructing a rare Hampden bomber which played an important role in the skies during the Second World War

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Julie and Debbie are both interested in rustling up healthy meals they can make without spending hours in the kitchen. Chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill come up with a couple of ideas, while Rylan Clark-Neal makes sure things don't get too boring

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Carpenter Will Kirk puts his carving skills to the test as he tackles a Scandinavian folk figurine that has been mauled by a mischievous puppy. Julie and Amanda work on a 1960s stuffed monkey who has seen better days, and Dominic faces the heavy duty task of unseizing a rusty old metal vice

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts this celebrity edition of the kitchen duel, which sees actor Stephen Mangan squaring up against barrister and TV judge Robert Rinder in a battle of the pots and pans. Paired with chefs Jeremy Pang and Ellis Barrie, they must create dishes from their £5.00 bag of ingredients in just 20 minutes - and with everything from a spicy noodle soup to a massive mushroom vol-au-vent on offer, it promises to be a close call

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants prepare their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two cooks, but also a MasterChef apron. The remaining trio take part in the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. The stakes are high as at the end, one contender will be sent home. Arbitrators in this edition include 2007 champion Steven Wallis and 2006 finalist Dean Edwards

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on people's expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Documentary uncovering the taboos surrounding surrogacy, following the experiences of five British women all trying to have babies for other people. They include single mum Caitlin, who has offered to help her boss Kate, who has lost three pregnancies, and 23-year-old Emma, who is pregnant with the baby of a gay couple she met on a fertility matching app. The programme also features David, a single gay man hoping to have a child through surrogacy

programme.title: Ovie: Life After Reality TV
programme.desc: Documentary following professional basketball player Ovie Soko. In the wake of an appearance on Love Island, he has become popular with fans away from the sport and is now juggling his time between basketball practice and his new celebrity life. But he realises he may soon have to decide which direction to take his life

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A tenant making over a £100,000 unlawfully sub-letting his council flat in London. Plus, efforts to rescue over 150 dogs being kept in squalid conditions at an animal centre in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and finds out how the village of Portmeirion became the birthplace of some famous pottery

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel has a final chance to persuade Maisie to end the hunger strike, as Jimmi finds out some disturbing news about the university. Luca tries to help two sisters who are at odds over how to care for their father as he struggles with worsening dementia

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is on a property-hunting mission spanning Somerset and Devon's Exmoor National Park with a couple who have a £550,000 budget and a love of carpentry and the coast. Along the way, he visits the Wool Marketing Board's fleece-sorting depot in South Molton, which grades and sells sheep wool from across the south-west region, and which contributes to Britain's £35million wool industry each year

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glasgow friends Simon and Carolyn are the chief cooks in both their households. They know each other's strengths and weaknesses well and are keen to see whether that will help or hinder them in Rylan Clark-Neal's fast-and-furious kitchen contest. They are joined in the Green Kitchen by queen of spice Romy Gill, while Anna Haugh brings a modern British and Irish twist to the Red Kitchen

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical expert Steve Fletcher takes on an ornate but broken clock, while metal expert Dominic Chinea tries to rejuvenate a toy carriage dating from 1911. Silversmith Brenton West carefully restores two platinum framed records given to rock star Jimmy McCulloch, a guitarist in Paul McCartney's band Wings

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the hospital board meet to make their decision about Ash's future, Suki has one more trick up her sleeve. Sharon's leaving party is unexpectedly quiet, but it proves the perfect opportunity for Kim to sign people up to her dating services. Mitch realises his efforts were in vain as he tries to do the right thing

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the most sensational break-up in recent political history. Once close friends and allies, Alex Salmond and Nicola Sturgeon took the SNP from nowhere to government, and Scotland to the brink of independence. Now sworn enemies, former First Minister Salmond accuses some of those he once led of plotting to have him imprisoned. Reporter Mark Daly investigates and asks what the split could mean for the future of Scotland, and for the United Kingdom

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The third quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by infamously hard task-master Jay Rayner - is to prepare a dessert where stone fruit is the star. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. His trek begins at the Millennium Bridge in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ultimately heading to Brands Hatch in Kent. Before he starts, the 21-year-old receives words of encouragement from Jose Mourinho, manager of his beloved Tottenham Hotspur, and along the route is joined by a host of celebrity supporters including Cheryl, Zoe Ball, Chris Ramsey, Dan Walker and Vick Hope

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and David Harper help teams at the East of England Showground in Peterborough, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp learns about a collection of fountain pens

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Jeremy Pang and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by friends Paul and Roberto, who have high-pressure jobs and like to barbecue together to relax

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Designers Manoj Malde and Tom Massey come up with alternative ideas for the West Sussex garden of wheelchair user Tracey, who can only venture into certain parts of her current space with assistance, and her husband Jake, who dreams of having a low-maintenance plot that he doesn't have to keep looking neat and tidy. Plus, Joel Bird helps a Liverpool couple turn their tiny, dull courtyard into a colourful paradise. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Music takes centre stage today as popstar Gracey talks about songwriting

programme.title: Great Expectations
programme.desc: Herbert Pocket takes Pip under his wing and tries to turn him into a gentleman, but the youngster's expensive taste sees him racking up debts, and his spending spirals farther out of control when he meets the charming and confident baronet Bentley Drummle. Miss Havisham asks Pip to visit her at Satis House, keen to see his transformation first-hand. With Douglas Booth, Harry Lloyd and Gillian Anderson

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian visit Sint-Truiden in Belgium, where Danny finds an antique sewing machine and Eric finds out how modern bronzes are cast

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian meet for the final competition of the week, the showdown. With half their items going to auction, it remains to be seen whether they will be able to turn a profit at all. Eric gets passionate about pottery when he finds a Davenport dish and Danny risks it all at the auction

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: A family home with an industrial twist, a sleek, split-level semi-detached property, and an eclectic Edwardian abode with a menagerie of surprises are all judged by neighbours

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum in Belfast, and also visits a family business that builds racing cars. With experts Mark Stacey and Caroline Hawley

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: A trip to Devon sees Simon Parfett and his team help Lucy and Rob, who are setting up their perfect B&B overlooking the Teign Valley in a '70s Californian airstream

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: The bikers head for Finland hoping for a change of pace from the intensity of St Petersburg, and they immediately fall in love with the tranquil and beautiful lakes. Dave learns how to enjoy a traditional Finnish sauna experience, while Si cooks up some Finnish pastries, before things take a more hectic turn as they ride tanks through the forest, learn about the national pastime of wife-carrying and meet a priest who has given his service a heavy metal twist

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As semi-final week kicks off, the four remaining designers are challenged to transform two restaurants in Hebden Bridge. Judge Michelle Ogundehin reveals that the competitors will be working in pairs, with each designer responsible for a single floor in the restaurant. Who will make it to the final?

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, triplets Peter, David and Phillip, who were adopted as young boys, have their DNA analysed by Professor Turi King as they attempt to explore their birth father's heritage. Meanwhile, a woman who was conceived from a sperm donor longs to learn about the other half of her ancestry, and find out if she has any half donor siblings

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is joined on the topical comedy show by guests Richard Madeley and Maisie Adam, as well as his mum and his hand-picked focus group of 20. Together they take a humorous look at the week's biggest talking points, from front page news to trending topics, helping the nation to navigate their way through these tricky times

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris Packham shines a light on some of the species that show how brainpower, ingenuity and clever tactics are just as important as brawn in the daily battle for survival. Spotted hyenas play politics to gain power, and the female with the most friends rules their group, while being a team player can help when it comes to getting a meal - as demonstrated by vampire bats, who share mouthfuls of blood with strangers in the hope of building a bond and receiving the favour in return

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise and fall of John Z DeLorean and his doomed attempt to build the sports car of the future in 1980s war-torn Belfast. The programme features rare and unseen footage filmed by Oscar winning directors DA Pennebaker and Chris Hegedus, offering the first in-depth psychological profile of DeLorean

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: In a special programme to end this series, Mary is in conversation with British writer Jed Mercurio. He's the mastermind behind hit show Line of Duty, returning for a new series in the spring, and has a rich back catalogue of attention-grabbing TV drama including Bodyguard, Cardiac Arrest and Bodies. How much does his early medical and military training inform his writing? And how much drama does it take to make real events into unmissable television?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return with the cookery competition as 40 contestants are put through gruelling culinary challenges to test not just their skills, but also their knowledge of food and ability to cope in a professional kitchen. They have to cook not only for their place in the competition but also for the right to wear a coveted MasterChef apron, with the first five contestants beginning by preparing their signature dish

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah meets a couple from Warwickshire who have a dream of owning a property in north-west Wales that would provide them with a home and the ability to offer holiday lets

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Hertfordshire friends Adam and Dan both follow a vegan diet, so today's ingredients for Ellis Barrie and Jeremy Pang include everything from vegan staples to more exotic produce. They both hope to walk away with some great new recipes - but who will leave as the winner? Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the culinary contest

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The designers work alone for the first time in the contest, each being assigned to transform a beach hut at Walton-on-the-Naze in Essex, in just two days on a budget of £1000. Working in a small space presents a serious challenge, and while some designers decide to incorporate existing features in their vision, others ruthlessly strip the huts bare in a bid to make their mark. Helping Michelle Ogundehin judge the results is journalist and interior designer Sophie Robinson

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Visual effects are the focus of today's episode, with behind-the-scenes secrets from His Dark Materials

programme.title: Great Expectations
programme.desc: Pip is devastated to learn the truth from Magwitch and rushes to Satis House, where he hears shocking news about Estella and discovers Miss Havisham has been encouraging his love for her adopted daughter out of cruelty. Wemmick warns him that rumours of Magwitch's return from Australia are rife, so he decides to help the convict evade recapture. Starring Douglas Booth, Ray Winstone, Vanessa Kirby and Gillian Anderson

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: James Braxton takes on Kate Bliss at an auction house in Kent, with the pair bidding on items including a dog bed and ocular objets d'art

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and James Braxton face-off at a car boot sale in Lincolnshire, and while she is baffled by a peculiar trumpet, James stumbles onto a Victorian iron press

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in St Albans - including a country lodge, a 1970s house, and an ultra-modern family home - are in the spotlight

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A look at some of the best finds by experts Thomas Plant, Adam Partridge, James Lewis, Mark Stacey and others during the programme's visits to Edinburgh, Wallasey, Southwell and Dunster Castle. Items featured include a collection of war medals and a unique funeral invitation. Plus, Paul Martin takes a tour of the Library of Birmingham and discovers what it has brought to the city

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett travels to the Blackdown Hills on the Somerset-Devon border, where he turns a 1960s trailer into a funky festival-themed accommodation

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: The bikers sample cuisine in Sweden, home to some of the world's top chefs, and cross the Arctic Circle into Lapland, where they learn about the basic cooking techniques used by the Sami people. They are introduced to the traditional techniques of smoking to cure meat, manufacturing hunting knives, and fishing to stock up for the coming winter. Using some local ingredients, Si and Dave treat their hosts to a berry and almond rice pudding and prepare their own Nordic reindeer and fish dishes

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The Wyoming Wetlands Society is a non-profit organisation that relocates beavers that have been damaging expensive landscaped areas and flooding yards and roads with their dam-building activities in Teton County. This documentary follows the work of Drew Reed as he traps the problem animals and takes them to drainage areas of the Gros Ventre River, where the beavers help to restore and enhance wetland habitats, providing many direct and indirect benefits to both wildlife and people. Rob Brydon narrates

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: A cunning attack on the ships proves the men are not battling an ordinary bear and that the region's Inuit culture may hold a key to their survival. Back in England, Franklin's wife and niece put pressure on the Admiralty to organise a rescue mission - and when they are refused, they decide to fund one themselves

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: The weather outside the ships continues to worsen, with severe storms and plummeting temperatures. On board Erebus, Goodsir speaks with Lady Silence in an effort to learn her language but has little success finding out about the nature of the creature. Crozier becomes temperamental as his liquor supplies run out and sends Edward to pilfer bottles of whiskey from Fitzjames' private stores

programme.title: Wild Scotland
programme.desc: Scottish wildlife cameraman and presenter Gordon Buchanan explores his native Mull and some of the nearby islands, filming otters, deer, puffins, seals and a minke whale

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at London's Shangri-La Hotel, Britain's highest hostelry. Their expectations of a standard business guest experience are blown away by discovering a hotel that makes guests' personal moments special. From bath butlers to marriage proposals, the place is a resort for celebrations in the clouds

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The art and cultural historian brings to life the huge personalities and larger-than-life stories behind each find - and reveals the surprising fact that man's understanding of each treasure is still shaped by the interpretations of these men and their eras, whether that's the height of the British Empire, or Europe in the grip of turn-of-the-20th century nationalistic fervour. In this episode, Janina heads to the blue seas and ancient palaces of the Greek island of Crete to discover the truth behind the myth of the minotaur

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Five more contenders compete for a place in the quarter finals. In the first round, they must prepare their signature dish, while the second sees them preparing a meal involving their favourite ingredient. One contestant will be eliminated in these two rounds, leaving the others to go through to the final challenge - preparing a two-course meal for judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode and past champions Saliha Mahmood Ahmed and Kenny Tutt

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith visits the Colne Valley Regional Park, a landscape of forest, fields and waterways on the edge of west London. She does battle with an invasive species, gets up close with tiny creatures that show the health of the park's rivers, meets a savvy septuagenarian saving her farm from development, and helps secure a future for one of our most endangered mammals, the water vole. Tom Heap asks whether eco-education should be at the heart of our school system, Adam Henson introduces new boar George II to his sows, and wildlife cameraman Jack Perks gets up close with otters

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a dog groomer who wants to relocate her business to Suffolk on a budget of £320,000. He also meets the team behind an ambitious project to restore an Anglo-Saxon longship, the remains of which were unearthed by an amateur archaeologist in the 1930s

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Julie and Debbie are both interested in rustling up healthy meals they can make without spending hours in the kitchen. Chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill come up with a couple of ideas, while Rylan Clark-Neal makes sure things don't get too boring

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces the unique talents of the world's most intelligent animals, investigating how nature's masterminds measure up against human brain power. Looking at a variety of animals, such as ravens, crows, bees and starlings, Chris explores how different animals can use tools, solve complex puzzles, recognise themselves in mirrors and even reason like humans

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Artist Ricky Martin on creating your own cartoon, plus more Big Songs

programme.title: Charles Dickens and the Invention of Christmas
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones reveals how Charles Dickens virtually invented the idea of a traditional family Christmas through one of his books, A Christmas Carol. When it was published in 1843, the story of Ebenezer Scrooge captured the imagination of Victorian Britain, and boosted the new craze for the festive season at a time when the idea of Santa Claus, mistletoe and crackers were relatively new

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss visit an antiques fair in Waterloo, Belgium, where James splashes out on an expensive portrait and Kate buys a rare toy aeroplane

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss continue their rivalry at an antiques fair in Peterborough, buying and selling collectibles for the most profit

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in St Albans - a converted farmyard barn with a modern twist, a zero-carbon self-build and a 1950s Scandinavian-style bungalow

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team pays a visit to Gloucester Cathedral, where experts Philip Serrell and Michael Baggott discover some rare gems among the antiques and collectibles brought for valuation, including a pre-Columbian burial vessel - one of the oldest items ever to have been featured on the show. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin marvels at the building's stained glass windows, one of which is as big as a tennis court

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and team head to the Forest of Dean, where they help Jon and Michelle to turn their old horse cart into a cosy and comfortable sanctuary for guests

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers sample the delicacies of a fisherwoman who had to fight for a place in the fishing community, before cooking meatballs with wild boar. The duo take DNA tests to find out if they have any Viking blood flowing through their veins, before heading for Stockholm where Dave takes a preserving masterclass with city gardener Johan and Si gets a pastry crash course with one of Sweden's youngest and most talented bakers. They conclude their road trip by taking a ferry to the medieval island of Gotland

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: The Secret Science of Sewage
programme.desc: Dr George McGavin, Dr Zoe Laughlin and a team of world-leading scientists explore the biology, chemistry, natural history and social history of faeces from a TV-base camp like no other - within the pools, tanks and bowels of one of the UK's largest and most technologically advanced sewage works

programme.title: Rob Brydon's Now That's What I Call Comic Relief
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Rob Brydon takes a look at the funniest, sweetest and silliest musical sketches and performances from the Comic Relief store cupboard

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: New discoveries from the edge of the solar system are transforming what is known about Pluto, thanks to the New Horizons space probe that took the first-ever close up images of the planet. Pluto was once thought to be geologically dead, but the pictures revealed it to be an active world of stunning complexity, with mountains carved from ice, a nitrogen glacier that appeared to be moving and a recently active volcano, with data sent back lead some scientists to speculate that there may even be life on Pluto today

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. In the first episode, she comes to the aid of Richard, who wants to learn if a man claiming to be his biological father is the genuine article, and Charlie, who wants to know what her chances are of contracting the same terminal illness as her father

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the four talented heat-winners return to fight for a place in knockout week in the Critic's Test. Their brief - set by Grace Dent, one of the country's toughest food critics - is to cook their take on a great British pudding. Presented by Gregg Wallace and John Torode

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste hear from a man who started an online relationship with a woman in France, whose incessant pleas for money turned to horrifying threats

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best continues his quest to uncover and explore lost railways, hearing about the astronomer who fought to keep the railway out of Armagh and the mill owner who paid to have one built. He also ventures into the dark reaches of Ireland's longest railway tunnel

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a landscape featuring all his trademarks - a lake, tall trees and a cabin with a majestic mountain in the background

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Hacker is challenged to convince Sir Humphrey that a computer holding vital citizen information could raise concerns about people's rights to privacy. Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne star

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Elizabeth has to stay with her ailing mother and tells Reggie to cancel a dinner party they have arranged - but he comes up with a better idea. Leonard Rossiter, Pauline Yates and John Barron star

programme.title: Ireland's Treasures Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary about the Irish artefacts that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland, both north and south. Alice Roberts and Gavin Hughes uncover the tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others

programme.title: Crock of Gold: A Few Rounds with Shane MacGowan (2020)
programme.desc: Director Julien Temple explores the wild life of beloved punk poet Shane MacGowan, the frontman of popular Anglo-Irish Celtic punk band the Pogues, who found fame in the 1980s and 1990s. The film combines interviews, archive footage and animations as it charts the singer's early days in Ireland, his time on the streets of London, where he embraced the punk scene, and his time with the Pogues and beyond. At the heart of the film is a new interview with MacGowan, revealing his passions, humour and deep knowledge of music, history, spirituality and popular culture

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Believing that Kalle is in danger, Mikko traces his phone and rushes to a neighbouring island where he finds and accosts Leo. Kalle, who has overheard his uncle's confession, tells Mikko who really killed Tommi. In Finnish

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Ellu admits to Tytti that he doesn't think that her mother likes him as Daniel drives Anna to their surprise weekend away. In Finnish

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a landscape featuring all his trademarks - a lake, tall trees and a cabin with a majestic mountain in the background

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best continues his quest to uncover and explore lost railways, hearing about the astronomer who fought to keep the railway out of Armagh and the mill owner who paid to have one built. He also ventures into the dark reaches of Ireland's longest railway tunnel

programme.title: Ireland's Treasures Uncovered

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best hears how the Fermanagh railways were used in a game of wartime espionage, takes a trip to the barbers and tells the surprising story of Belleek's female admiral

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a wintry scene in which the glistening sun peeks through the trees to warm the cold day

programme.title: Armada - 12 Days to Save England
programme.desc: Dan Snow concludes the three-part drama-documentary exploring the battle against the Spanish Armada. The final episode reveals the circumstances that led to the defeat of the invasion force, drawing on recently rediscovered documents, and also explores how victory enhanced Elizabeth I's reputation as a world leader - while many of the sailors who fought in the battle died of starvation and disease. Featuring dramatic reconstructions, starring Anita Dobson as Elizabeth

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: King Philip prepares to launch the Armada to avenge Mary's execution and Elizabeth pins her hopes on peace talks with Spain, but Drake's actions in Cadiz force her hand

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: The docudrama focuses on the machinations of ambitious aristocrat the Earl of Essex as he tries to take over Robert Cecil's spy network in an attempt to gain control over the ageing Elizabeth I and hold the power to choose the next king of England

programme.title: Some People with Jokes
programme.desc: People from the Emerald Isle tell their favourite gags to the camera in a bid to get the public laughing with wisecracks old and new

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright narrates a St Patrick's Day special, featuring archive performances by the Boomtown Rats, the Corrs, Enya, Shane MacGowan and Johnny Depp, Stiff Little Fingers, Thin Lizzy, Sinead O'Connor and U2

programme.title: Songs of Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians including Brian Kennedy, Cara Dillon, Finbar Furey, Eleanor McEvoy and Luka Bloom perform contemporary and traditional songs from their native Ireland at the Royal Concert Hall, Glasgow, as part of the 2012 Celtic Connections festival. The singers are backed by Irish-American group Solas. Hosted by Ricky Ross

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge discovers the secret role that neutral Ireland played assisting the Allies in the Second World War

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which adventurer Paul Rose explores the 79 miles of the Yorkshire Wolds Way, one of Britain's least well-known national walking trails. The trail starts at the Humber Estuary and ends at the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Filey. On the way, Paul takes in the views from the top of the Humber Bridge, learns to ride a penny farthing and searches out the spots made internationally famous by the artist David Hockney

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's luck takes a sudden turn for the better in his romance with Helen, but there is no respite for the unfortunate Dalby family. Tristan's capacity for causing chaos shows no sign of coming to an end

programme.desc: Following his mother's death, Luke, a young man with Down's syndrome, moves into a care home. After sneaking out of the house to explore the countryside, he meets a young woman who gives him hope and purpose. Drama, starring Darren Kent, Steven Brandon, Suzanna Hamilton and Pixie Le Knot

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines the coming-of-age film, and explains why it is such a powerful and popular medium for exploring the tumult and transformations of adolescence. Featured movies include The 400 Blows, The Breakfast Club, Kes and Lady Bird

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens reveals how, as the 20th century dawned, artists found radical new ways to depict Wales, pushing themselves to extremes and redefining what art could be. His venture takes him to the National Museum Wales in Cardiff, Bardsey Island off the Llyn Peninsula, Colwyn Bay and Swansea

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank's journey takes him through Uzbekistan, Iran and Syria, where he finds the local people far more welcoming to Western travellers than expected. Among the locations he visits are the Fire Temple of Azerbaijan, the Trading Domes of Uzbekistan, the Bisitun carvings at the top of a cliff face in Iran, and the ancient city of Persepolis

Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There has been a special sleep over event at Safari World, where visitors had the chance to see all the nocturnal animals. Andy heads to the Aqua Base to find out how it went. Jen informs Andy that it has been a huge success and that Mr Hammond is just taking the visitors to see the last of the animals, the cephalopods. Mr Hammond calls and is still wearing his stripy pyjamas. He is having trouble spotting the common octopus, which disappeared the moment she saw him. Jen uses a special sonar device, which can detect objects under the water, but it is not able to detect the missing octopus. If they can find out where octopus like to hide in the wild, maybe that will help them to find the missing octopus at Safari World. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! An

Gail’s team turn to the Crystal Ball for help. In Nana’s Panto Showdown, Nana Knickerbocker and her cows are busy making pizzas. After the Crystal Ball refuses to help, Gail’s team are still yet to score a gigglepoint at the halfway point in the show. Round 3 features a clip starring King Flannel trying to get a biscuit. After some more refusal from the Crystal Ball, Wizard Tripwick and Gail struggle to answer the questions themselves. Keith produces his own crystal football to help his team with the answers. After saying goodbye to the Crystal Ball and totting up the final scores, the winners of this GiggleQuiz are revealed.

programme.desc: It’s a big day at the Hidden Lake for one new arrival. Joe the Mallard Duckling hatched from his egg last night, and his mum is taking him for his very first swim. He takes to it like a duck to water, so it’s not long before his excitement gets the better of him, and he’s zooming through the water like a speedboat. Despite his mother’s warnings, Joe zooms faster and faster, until he’s zoomed so much he finds himself lost! The Hidden Lake is a big place for a small duckling. Can he use all his courage and zooming skills to find his way back to mum?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Zahra-Rose's family is from Somalia, a country situated on the east coast of Africa. In Somalia, they have the traditional dish of nafaqo, which is boiled egg wrapped in mashed potato. This is what Zahra-Rose has chosen to make for her friends. To begin with, Zahra-Rose seasons mashed potato with salt, black pepper, flour, ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric powder and fresh coriander. She then wraps the potato mixture around her special ingredient, hard-boiled eggs, rolls them in breadcrumbs and puts them in the oven to be cooked. Zahra-Rose also serves a fresh chilli sauce, so her friends can use it for dipping. Not only does Zahra-Rose have beautiful decorations, she also has a surprise picnic planned. Will her friends realise what her special ingredient is?

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There has been a big tropical storm at Safari World and Andy has made sure all the animals are safe and well. The storm has damaged the electricity network. Mr Hammond explains that he has been checking on all the bird’s nests, but there is one nest he is not able to find: the nest of a storm petrel, which is a type of sea bird. Mr Hammond is worried that their nest may have blown away. Andy decides to call Jen to check the park’s database to find out where they nest. Unfortunately, due to the power outages, the database is not working! It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Emily and Matthew as they discover the world around them at five months old and get stronger every day. Emily has a fun day exploring the great outdoors with her brother, sister, mum and dad. They start their day by playing with a ball, rolling it backwards and forwards between them. Emily even manages to push it away too! Meanwhile, Matthew and his family go to a local play cafe where they have fun in a colourful ball pit. Once Emily's family head out for a walk, they find a beautiful waterfall and stream and decide to have a boat race - but who will come out as the winner? When Matthew arrives at a park, he gets the chance to ride a steam train and find out how they make it go! We then follow Emily as she attends a Baby yoga class with her mum and sister. They learn how to strengthen those baby muscles before singing some songs and bouncing on a giant yoga ball. Matthew finishes his day by going down to the beach to help his siblings build a sandcastle.

The dough is formed and rolled into ovals and placed on a baking tray. The second is made up of red peppers and his special ingredient, halloumi. They are put onto skewers with an oil, oregano, honey and lemon marinade and put into the oven with his pitta bread. Emilio has made a Cypriot wishing tree for his friends to tie their wishes to. But will they like his recipe when they try it?

Plus, we find out why ladybirds are great for the environment. They are pest controllers, who eat lots of little bugs that can destroy valuable crops.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. After a tropical rain during the night at Safari World, Andy has been making sure all the animals are safe. Apart from being a little damp, thankfully they’re all okay. Mr Hammond calls Andy and Jen worried that slugs might be eating the giant lettuce he is growing specially for the Galapagos tortoises. Jen and Andy check the cameras and thankfully the slugs have not got to the lettuce – well, not yet at any rate. Jen knows that putting broken shells on the soil around plants deters slugs and snails from eating them. She then discovers on the park’s database that a fish called a tusk fish that has a collection of broken shells. The only problem is, there are no tusk fish at Safari World. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Andy boards his safari-sub, a hi-t

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch supersizes a fierce Komodo dragon, and the Go Jetters must find the sizeray to fix the glitch

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Ann Teak and Professor Muddles join team captains Keith Fitt and Gail Force for funny clips and questions, but strange things soon start to happen as Professor Muddles investigates a mystery machine from Arthur’s lost-and-found box. Round 1 is a simple clip round with a question for each team. Gail and Professor Muddles are up first. Arthur reveals a dance clip starring Dan Step, but can they remember the picture on Dan Step’s basket? Keith and Ann Teak are next. This time it’s a clip starring our very own Gail Force at a running race, but can Keith and Ann remember the trophy that Gail was holding? With Round 1 over, Arthur decides to put the trophies back in the lost-and-found box, where he got them. He’s amazed at the stuff that people leave lying around, like a rubber duck, a lamb teddy, and a mysterious and complicated-looking machine. Gail and Muddles are fascinated by the device. Round 2 kicks off with Nana Knickerbocker’s Panto Showdown, featuring Nana’s cows attempting to make

programme.title: Roman Kemp: Our Silent Emergency
programme.desc: Last August, Roman Kemp's life changed dramatically with the sudden and unexpected death of his best friend, radio producer Joe Lyons. It led him to question why increasing numbers of young men are taking their own lives and why so many of them never ask for help. Here, Roman sets out to explore what can be done to encourage people to seek help, what preventative action others need to be taking, and the lasting impact of mental health and suicide on friends and family

programme.title: Murray Walker: A Life in the Fast Lane
programme.desc: A profile of the motor-racing commentator whose voice became one of the most recognisable on British television. programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Jez and Ieuz have one time for one last masterclass in Napoli before leaving for Parma. There they pick up a final sprinkling of parmesan, mix the most important batch of dough they will ever make and try to get a good night's sleep before the competition. Then destiny awaits as they face the best pizzaiolos on the planet at the Pizza World Championship

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants prepare their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two cooks, but also a MasterChef apron. Arbitrators in this edition include 2007 champion Steven Wallis and 2006 finalist Dean Edwards

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on people's expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Documentary uncovering the taboos surrounding surrogacy, following the experiences of five British women all trying to have babies for other people. They include single mum Caitlin, who has offered to help her boss Kate, who has lost three pregnancies, and 23-year-old Emma, who is pregnant with the baby of a gay couple she met on a fertility matching app. The programme also features David, a single gay man hoping to have a child through surrogacy

programme.title: Ovie: Life After Reality TV
programme.desc: Documentary following professional basketball player Ovie Soko. In the wake of an appearance on Love Island, he has become popular with fans away from the sport and is now juggling his time between basketball practice and his new celebrity life. But he realises he may soon have to decide which direction to take his life

programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel has a final chance to persuade Maisie to end the hunger strike, as Jimmi finds out some disturbing news about the university. Luca tries to help two sisters who are at odds over how to care for their father as he struggles with worsening dementia

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is on a property-hunting mission spanning Somerset and Devon's Exmoor National Park with a couple who have a £550,000 budget and a love of carpentry and the coast. Along the way, he visits the Wool Marketing Board's fleece-sorting depot in South Molton, which grades and sells sheep wool from across the south-west region, and which contributes to Britain's £35million wool industry each year

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glasgow friends Simon and Carolyn are the chief cooks in both their households. They know each other's strengths and weaknesses well and are keen to see whether that will help or hinder them in Rylan Clark-Neal's fast-and-furious kitchen contest. They are joined in the Green Kitchen by queen of spice Romy Gill, while Anna Haugh brings a modern British and Irish twist to the Red Kitchen

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical expert Steve Fletcher takes on an ornate but broken clock, while metal expert Dominic Chinea tries to rejuvenate a toy carriage dating from 1911. Silversmith Brenton West carefully restores two platinum framed records given to rock star Jimmy McCulloch, a guitarist in Paul McCartney's band Wings

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas gear up for the Six Nations as Wales travel to face France in the deciding round of fixtures. Their guests are Welsh hero Shane Williams, World Cup-winner Maggie Alphonsi, Paralympic legend Tanni Grey-Thompson and French fly-half Thomas Castaignede

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the hospital board meet to make their decision about Ash's future, Suki has one more trick up her sleeve. Sharon's leaving party is unexpectedly quiet, but it proves the perfect opportunity for Kim to sign people up to her dating services. Mitch realises his efforts were in vain as he tries to do the right thing

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the most sensational break-up in recent political history. Once close friends and allies, Alex Salmond and Nicola Sturgeon took the SNP from nowhere to government, and Scotland to the brink of independence. Now sworn enemies, former First Minister Salmond accuses some of those he once led of plotting to have him imprisoned. Reporter Mark Daly investigates and asks what the split could mean for the future of Scotland, and for the United Kingdom

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The third quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by infamously hard task-master Jay Rayner - is to prepare a dessert where stone fruit is the star. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. His trek begins at the Millennium Bridge in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ultimately heading to Brands Hatch in Kent. Before he starts, the 21-year-old receives words of encouragement from Jose Mourinho, manager of his beloved Tottenham Hotspur, and along the route is joined by a host of celebrity supporters including Cheryl, Zoe Ball, Chris Ramsey, Dan Walker and Vick Hope

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective

programme.desc: Daniel has a final chance to persuade Maisie to end the hunger strike, as Jimmi finds out some disturbing news about the university. Luca tries to help two sisters who are at odds over how to care for their father as he struggles with worsening dementia

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is on a property-hunting mission spanning Somerset and Devon's Exmoor National Park with a couple who have a £550,000 budget and a love of carpentry and the coast. Along the way, he visits the Wool Marketing Board's fleece-sorting depot in South Molton, which grades and sells sheep wool from across the south-west region, and which contributes to Britain's £35million wool industry each year

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glasgow friends Simon and Carolyn are the chief cooks in both their households. They know each other's strengths and weaknesses well and are keen to see whether that will help or hinder them in Rylan Clark-Neal's fast-and-furious kitchen contest. They are joined in the Green Kitchen by queen of spice Romy Gill, while Anna Haugh brings a modern British and Irish twist to the Red Kitchen

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical expert Steve Fletcher takes on an ornate but broken clock, while metal expert Dominic Chinea tries to rejuvenate a toy carriage dating from 1911. Silversmith Brenton West carefully restores two platinum framed records given to rock star Jimmy McCulloch, a guitarist in Paul McCartney's band Wings

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the hospital board meet to make their decision about Ash's future, Suki has one more trick up her sleeve. Sharon's leaving party is unexpectedly quiet, but it proves the perfect opportunity for Kim to sign people up to her dating services. Mitch realises his efforts were in vain as he tries to do the right thing

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the most sensational break-up in recent political history. Once close friends and allies, Alex Salmond and Nicola Sturgeon took the SNP from nowhere to government, and Scotland to the brink of independence. Now sworn enemies, former First Minister Salmond accuses some of those he once led of plotting to have him imprisoned. Reporter Mark Daly investigates and asks what the split could mean for the future of Scotland, and for the United Kingdom

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The third quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by infamously hard task-master Jay Rayner - is to prepare a dessert where stone fruit is the star. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. His trek begins at the Millennium Bridge in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ultimately heading to Brands Hatch in Kent. Before he starts, the 21-year-old receives words of encouragement from Jose Mourinho, manager of his beloved Tottenham Hotspur, and along the route is joined by a host of celebrity supporters including Cheryl, Zoe Ball, Chris Ramsey, Dan Walker and Vick Hope

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective

programme.desc: Louise Minchin and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather
