programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Martin Roberts look around properties in Blackpool, Derby and the West Midlands town of Bilston, before hearing how they have been redeveloped

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers keep an eye out for coach drivers who refuse to take a break despite being exhausted, and track down two fraudsters running a sim swap scam

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and David Harper help two teams buy items at Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits the Museum of Timekeeping in Newark

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel tries his best to end the hunger strike once and for all. A young woman in Ruhma's care is warned not to eat during labour but her overbearing father has other ideas

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a dog groomer who wants to relocate her business to Suffolk on a budget of £320,000. He also meets the team behind an ambitious project to restore an Anglo-Saxon longship, the remains of which were unearthed by an amateur archaeologist in the 1930s

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Hertfordshire friends Adam and Dan both follow a vegan diet, so today's ingredients for Ellis Barrie and Jeremy Pang include everything from vegan staples to more exotic produce. They both hope to walk away with some great new recipes - but who will leave as the winner? Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the culinary contest

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Toy restorers Amanda and Julie revive a sad dog that has been badly burned in a devastating house fire, metal expert Dominic tries to get a rusty retro kitchen mixer beating again and silversmith Brenton works his magic on a nurse's belt buckle

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon asks Kathy how Gavin died and is shocked when she realises Kathy is planning to go to the memorial service - as far as she is concerned, he may have been her biological father but he can rot in hell. Peter finds himself caught in the middle when Ash and Suki argue, and Bailey finds a stray dog - but Karen says they cannot afford to keep it

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki tells Ash she should do anything necessary to get her job back, so Peter assures her he will say he gave himself the medication. Mitch explains that Bailey is heartbroken over losing Bronson so Rainie enlists Stuart's help to find the stray dog. As an emotional Sharon packs up her things up in the Vic, Kim tries to organise her a leaving party

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira tells Hanssen that he may not have been Reyhan's only victim, leading the great surgeon to become distracted while operating on her father. Chloe realises Ange is missing and grows increasingly suspicious that Cameron has something to do with her disappearance. Louis gets into an altercation with a former friend who has turned up seeking revenge

programme.title: Roman Kemp: Our Silent Emergency
programme.desc: Last August, Roman Kemp's life changed dramatically with the sudden and unexpected death of his best friend, radio producer Joe Lyons. It led him to question why increasing numbers of young men are taking their own lives and why so many of them never ask for help. Here, Roman sets out to explore what can be done to encourage people to seek help, what preventative action others need to be taking, and the lasting impact of mental health and suicide on friends and family

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Jennifer O'Leary investigates one of Northern Ireland's largest criminal gangs - one which trades in drugs and murder but stands under the banner of loyalism - South East Antrim UDA

programme.title: Murray Walker: A Life in the Fast Lane
programme.desc: A profile of the motor-racing commentator whose voice became one of the most recognisable on British television. The programme follows Murray as he travels to the 2011 Australian Grand Prix, looks back at highlights from his career on radio and TV and recalls his time in the Army. Featuring contributions by Nigel Mansell, Damon Hill, Michael Schumacher, Jenson Button and Lewis Hamilton

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Sheriff Romero responds to Norma's 911 call and has a surprising proposal for her concerning the deaths of Keith Summers and Zack Shelby. Even with those recent events now behind them, Norma reacts badly to Dylan's decision to move into his own place. The hotel is not quite open yet but gets its first guest Jake Abernathy, who claims to have been a regular when Summers owned the place

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is upset after Emma tells some of the other girls that he and Bradley slept together. He leaves school without permission, and in a meeting with the principal and his teacher it is suggested to Norma that Norman should seek professional help. The session with the psychologist does not go well as the patient shows signs of severe strain

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Louise Minchin and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a Victorian semi-detached house in Dresden, Stoke-on-Trent, a two-bedroom semi in Croydon in Surrey and public toilets in Kincardine, Fife

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers hunt for counterfeit and dangerous goods at Suffolk's Felixstowe Port, and track down a gang who callously dumped tons of illegal waste

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from Wetherby racecourse, West Yorkshire, joined by experts Richard Madley and Jonathan Pratt, and also pays a visit to the Royal Armouries in Leeds

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie embarks on a magnet fishing adventure, while Maisie challenges Daniel to put his money where his mouth is. A work experience student tries to hide her ethnic background but an emergency with her father forces her to reconsider

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in the Shropshire countryside to help a couple who have £425,000 to invest in a character home that gives them space for entertaining and their beloved jukebox. Away from the house search, Ginny visits the Royal Air Force Museum Cosford and meets the conservation team reconstructing a rare Hampden bomber which played an important role in the skies during the Second World War

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Julie and Debbie are both interested in rustling up healthy meals they can make without spending hours in the kitchen. Chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill come up with a couple of ideas, while Rylan Clark-Neal makes sure things don't get too boring

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Carpenter Will Kirk puts his carving skills to the test as he tackles a Scandinavian folk figurine that has been mauled by a mischievous puppy. Julie and Amanda work on a 1960s stuffed monkey who has seen better days, and Dominic faces the heavy duty task of unseizing a rusty old metal vice

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best reaches Lough Foyle, the final meeting point of all the rivers and streams and tributaries in the Foyle network. He takes a sailing lesson up the lough, goes in search of sharks and meets one-armed sailor Garry Crothers, who helmed a one-man boat all the way home from the Caribbean

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants prepare their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two cooks, but also a MasterChef apron. The remaining trio take part in the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. The stakes are high as at the end, one contender will be sent home. Arbitrators in this edition include 2007 champion Steven Wallis and 2006 finalist Dean Edwards

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on people's expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.title: Shane Todd's Lockdown Rave
programme.desc: Shane and Dave Elliott bring their hit radio show to TV for one night only to celebrate St Patrick's Day, featuring a selection of favourite dance tunes with plenty of shout-outs and craic

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Documentary uncovering the taboos surrounding surrogacy, following the experiences of five British women all trying to have babies for other people. They include single mum Caitlin, who has offered to help her boss Kate, who has lost three pregnancies, and 23-year-old Emma, who is pregnant with the baby of a gay couple she met on a fertility matching app. The programme also features David, a single gay man hoping to have a child through surrogacy

programme.title: Ovie: Life After Reality TV
programme.desc: Documentary following professional basketball player Ovie Soko. In the wake of an appearance on Love Island, he has become popular with fans away from the sport and is now juggling his time between basketball practice and his new celebrity life. But he realises he may soon have to decide which direction to take his life

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As the government faces mounting criticism that well-connected people made millions out of Britain's PPE crisis, Panorama investigates who won out. More than £12billion was spent in the first six months of the pandemic on contracts to provide personal protective equipment. Reporter Richard Bilton meets one man who made £40million on a deal, and speaks to others who felt ignored in favour of less experienced suppliers. As the government refuses to reveal the full details of all its so-called VIP deals, the programme reveals the high-profile connections to one lucrative contract

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As the government faces mounting criticism that well-connected people made millions out of Britain's PPE crisis, Panorama investigates who won out. More than £12billion was spent in the first six months of the pandemic on contracts to provide personal protective equipment. Reporter Richard Bilton meets one man who made £40million on a deal, and speaks to others who felt ignored in favour of less experienced suppliers. As the government refuses to reveal the full details of all its so-called VIP deals, the programme reveals the high-profile connections to one lucrative contract

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith sneaks into the farm to find evidence. Cain reveals the truth to Moira. Will lies Harriet is away on a retreat

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a guide to some of the most beautiful sights Norfolk has to offer. Ben Fogle takes to the Broads on a traditional Norfolk wherry boat, while in the flat wetlands of the Fens, Liz Bonnin checks out some rare eight-legged wildlife. Elsewhere, Olympian Victoria Pendleton joins the Queens Mounted Regiment on their annual holiday at Holkham beach

programme.title: Bear Grylls Wild Adventure Specials
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes in which two sports stars test their survival instincts, former rugby union player Jonny Wilkinson joins Bear Grylls to explore Dartmoor's harsh and gruelling terrain, an adventure that will push his physical and mental limits. From climbing 40 metres up a sheer cliff face to crossing ice cold rivers, Jonny also learns the key basic survival skills necessary to survive in remote terrain like Dartmoor

programme.title: 2020: The Story of Us
programme.desc: Oscar-winning director Kevin Macdonald's feature-length documentary tells the story of coronavirus in Britain through the experience of people immersed in its impact, both personally and professionally. Using testimony from staff in hospitals - consultants, doctors and nurses - as well as weaving in the experience of patients, this documentary paints a vivid portrait of the working lives of ICU staff and those they treat during a crisis which proves both challenging, life-changing and often tragic for those caught in it

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has her work cut out as she tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Elsewhere, new cargo driver Dylan hopes his love for hypnosis can get him out of a jam

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kat is stung when Ronnie introduces her to Ed as his business partner. When Alina suggests they work on her car together, Tyrone is secretly thrilled. Cathy reveals she'd like to do more to help Steve with his fundraising, but Tracy gives her short shrift

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, TV personalities Amanda Holden and Alan Carr embark on a journey across the country as they delve into their family history, unlocking secrets of the past and meeting close family they never knew existed. Amanda's journey starts in the grounds of a stately home in Cornwall, while Alan's hunt for his ancestors takes him to a pub in Erith with something of a colourful past

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian takes a trip down memory lane as he presents a series of classic TV game shows, which have all been updated with a new final round offering the contestants a chance to win big. The next four weeks will see Alan in charge of The Price Is Right, Take Your Pick, Strike it Lucky and Bullseye. But tonight he steps into the shoes of much-missed funnyman Bruce Forsyth for Play Your Cards Right, which sees four celebrity couples taking a chance on the turn of a single card. The hopefuls are daytime stalwarts Ruth Langsford and Eamonn Holmes, comedian Chris Ramsey and his wife Rosie, presenter Ore Oduba and his wife Portia, and singer-actress Martine McCutcheon and her husband Jack McManus

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a guide to some of the most beautiful sights Norfolk has to offer. Ben Fogle takes to the Broads on a traditional Norfolk wherry boat, while in the flat wetlands of the Fens, Liz Bonnin checks out some rare eight-legged wildlife. Elsewhere, Olympian Victoria Pendleton joins the Queens Mounted Regiment on their annual holiday at Holkham beach

programme.title: Bear Grylls Wild Adventure Specials
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes in which two sports stars test their survival instincts, former rugby union player Jonny Wilkinson joins Bear Grylls to explore Dartmoor's harsh and gruelling terrain, an adventure that will push his physical and mental limits. From climbing 40 metres up a sheer cliff face to crossing ice cold rivers, Jonny also learns the key basic survival skills necessary to survive in remote terrain like Dartmoor

programme.title: 2020: The Story of Us
programme.desc: Oscar-winning director Kevin Macdonald's feature-length documentary tells the story of coronavirus in Britain through the experience of people immersed in its impact, both personally and professionally. Using testimony from staff in hospitals - consultants, doctors and nurses - as well as weaving in the experience of patients, this documentary paints a vivid portrait of the working lives of ICU staff and those they treat during a crisis which proves both challenging, life-changing and often tragic for those caught in it

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has her work cut out as she tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How a homemade guitar amp changed the course of music history overnight and how a team of expert pen makers are giving a Second World War fighter a new lease of life

programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, TV personalities Amanda Holden and Alan Carr embark on a journey across the country as they delve into their family history, unlocking secrets of the past and meeting close family they never knew existed. Amanda's journey starts in the grounds of a stately home in Cornwall, while Alan's hunt for his ancestors takes him to a pub in Erith with something of a colourful past

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian takes a trip down memory lane as he presents a series of classic TV game shows, which have all been updated with a new final round offering the contestants a chance to win big. The next four weeks will see Alan in charge of The Price Is Right, Take Your Pick, Strike it Lucky and Bullseye. But tonight he steps into the shoes of much-missed funnyman Bruce Forsyth for Play Your Cards Right, which sees four celebrity couples taking a chance on the turn of a single card. The hopefuls are daytime stalwarts Ruth Langsford and Eamonn Holmes, comedian Chris Ramsey and his wife Rosie, presenter Ore Oduba and his wife Portia, and singer-actress Martine McCutcheon and her husband Jack McManus

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz turns to Jim for help when she thinks everything is falling apart for her. Liam tells the Malletts they can have Katie back for 2,000 pounds

programme.desc: With Zoe gone, Ashley asks Maxine to move back in. Natalie attempts to make Kevin jealous. Zoe does not trust Liam and runs away from the squat, taking Shannon with her

programme.desc: The day of Vic's funeral arrives. Chris receives a summons to appear as Kim's defence witness. Ned threatens Eric when he gossips about the Sugdens

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak declares he wants to be a house husband. Viv cannot cope running the post office by herself. Mandy is jealous that Paddy and Tricia are getting on well

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass attempts to turn events to his advantage when a remote farm is raided, and a visitor from London evokes bad memories. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates come up with a new rule to give their romantic pursuits a chance of privacy - but it means giving 24 hours' notice. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick interrupts an armed robbery at the Aidensfield Arms and ends up being held hostage in the cellar with Greengrass. Meanwhile, the local newsagent is found to be selling Swedish pornography. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lyn starts work at the vets. Rachel gets an invitation to visit Steve at the remand centre. Kelly warns Terry to stay away from Viv. Laura invites Chris for a romantic meal

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Sarah worry about going back to the village. Zak and Butch take Belle to the betting shop. Kathy tells Kim she is unsure if Steve hit her deliberately or not

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is stunned when Jon tells her he cannot spend all of Christmas Day with her. The Battersbys attempt to talk Les out of killing their Christmas turkey

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary tells Judy he is thinking of leaving her. Les is furious to find out Toyah has freed the turkey. Liam tracks Zoe down and tells her his duplicitous plan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Barnaby is dragged along to the wedding of the son of Sarah's friend Hazel, the last thing he expects to find himself doing is investigating a murder. But when his wife discovers the body of the bride, Laurel, who has been squeezed to death by her own wedding dress corset, the detective must look more closely at his fellow guests. The victim's husband Gavin is convinced that his wife's death had everything to do with her outspoken nature on her radio show, so Winter's attention soon turns to her unique community of nocturnal listeners. Gavin's scorned ex-fiancee and local wedding dress designer Serena also comes under suspicion, and Barnaby and Winter's enquiries lead them to a local wedding fayre. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Kelly Brook, Nick Hancock and Liz Fraser

programme.title: Quiz
programme.desc: Charles returns for a second night in the hot seat with a new strategy. After his previously hopeless performance, he proves massively successful, his dithering answers invariably turning out to be right. However, the production team find his sudden change of fortunes suspicious, and begin looking into an unusual pattern of coughing from the studio audience

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a wealthy man whose double life left him vulnerable to exploitation. Away from work, Rachel's recent moment of indiscretion threatens to come back to haunt her when a confused Sean takes a phone call from her one-night stand, and after Ade is pushed into living with his girlfriend on a permanent basis, Janet asks her mother to move in

programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans returns as the young Inspector Morse in the first of four feature-length episodes. A baffling suicide, an anguished father searching for his missing daughter and the smash-and-grab robbery of medieval artefacts throw the detective in at the deep end as he resumes his role with Oxford City Police following a four-month absence from duty. As the locals go to the polls in a closely fought by-election and a beauty contest builds to its conclusion, Endeavour must navigate the choppy waters of both worlds as his investigation shakes the highest pillars of Oxford society. Co-starring Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw, Jack Laskey and Sean Rigby

programme.title: Quiz
programme.desc: Persecuted and hounded by the public and press, Charles, Diana and Tecwen head to court. Drama, starring Matthew Macfadyen and Sian Clifford

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Troubled saleswoman Helen (Nicola Walker), whose mother was murdered back in episode one, turns up on Janet's doorstep with information that takes the investigation in a completely different direction - paving the way for the formidable Det Supt Julie Dodson to be called in. Meanwhile, new recruit Sgt Rob Waddington arrives on the scene - putting Janet's nose out of joint as she is demoted back down to constable. Emmerdale star Danny Miller (Aaron Livesy) joins the cast

David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An Army deserter arrives in the village following a bust-up with his sadistic sergeant and causes problems for the Rowans when he starts stealing food to survive. The fugitive is only discovered when Nick investigates a series of break-ins around the village. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is questioned about the horse theft. Paddy suffers following his night out. Kim arranges a mystery rendezvous. Graham is angry with Rachel when she makes fun of him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim heads to Home Farm for a final confrontation with Chris to get her money back, and things take a violent turn. Graham storms out after an argument with Rachel

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally does not like the idea of Kevin working with Natalie. Judy is horrified when Leanne tells the Rovers' customers that Judy bought Zoe's baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin refuses to sell the garage and asks Sally to trust him. Deirdre is furious when Jon tells her he has to work on New Year's Eve. Liz is thrilled when Mike offers her a job

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Chief Superintendent Jack Cornish goes missing and a recently embalmed body is discovered near an isolated farm track - could there be a link? As Hathaway goes on sabbatical in Kosovo, Lewis takes an awkward young constable under his wing to investigate the case. Guest starring Lucy Speed and Peter Davison, with Kevin Whately and Babou Ceesay

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Unexplained killings rock the Bodmin community, giving rise to fears that the so-called 'beast', a monster enshrined in local legend, has returned to wreak fresh havoc. Kersey ventures up onto the moors to investigate and does not return - so Wycliffe is forced to go it alone. Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Having realised her marriage is a mistake, Rachel is still staying at Janet's and making excuses for her absence, but Sean isn't prepared to accept that things have gone so wrong, so quickly. Meanwhile, the investigation into Joe Bevan continues as the body count rises and the team faces the mammoth task of identifying the victims and determining the order in which they were murdered. Detective drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Sean Maguire

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Barnaby is dragged along to the wedding of the son of Sarah's friend Hazel, the last thing he expects to find himself doing is investigating a murder. But when his wife discovers the body of the bride, Laurel, who has been squeezed to death by her own wedding dress corset, the detective must look more closely at his fellow guests. The victim's husband Gavin is convinced that his wife's death had everything to do with her outspoken nature on her radio show, so Winter's attention soon turns to her unique community of nocturnal listeners. Gavin's scorned ex-fiancee and local wedding dress designer Serena also comes under suspicion, and Barnaby and Winter's enquiries lead them to a local wedding fayre. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Kelly Brook, Nick Hancock and Liz Fraser

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade turns his attention from freshwater mysteries to the world's oceans, going in search of the real creature behind the enduring sea serpent legend. His investigation leads him to Thailand where the mythology of the sea serpent reveals a vital clue, before he heads to the unlikely location of France where he tries to finally solve the mystery and have a sea serpent encounter of his own

Thriller sequel, with Charles Bronson, Lesley-Anne Down, Robert Joy and Michael Parks

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Riggs and Murtaugh investigate a murder inside a hospital of a prominent figure from the mob. Their main suspect is a doctor who supposedly died five years earlier, but who it now seems may have been in hiding from hoodlums since faking his demise. On a personal level, Murtaugh learns that his daughter Riana is secretly dating the son of his neighbour and nemesis Jim McNeile. Plus, Riggs spends more time with his childhood friend, Molly, and Scorsese's real name is revealed. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.desc: Templar rescues a Russian damsel in distress and agrees to help her fend off art thieves eager to get their hands on her da Vinci masterpieces. Cult adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A security guard is murdered during a payroll snatch, and Regan and Carter are called in to support local investigating officers. However, bad blood between the maverick detective inspector and the force's chief superintendent going back several years manifests itself in an old allegation of corruption being rekindled. Richard Griffiths features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman in the final episode of the crime drama

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at a company manufacturing the hand-sewn Union Jacks seen flying over Buckingham Palace, and a Devon forge that produces wood burning stoves. programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as one-time Coronation Street actor Jimi Mistry (Khalid Nazir), former Emmerdale star Gemma Atkinson (Carly Hope), Loose Women's Nadia Sawalha and veteran actor James Cosmo pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for the charity of their choice. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Step Brothers (2008)
programme.desc: Brennan and Dale are two 40-year-old men who have never flown the nest. All that changes when Brennan's mother marries Dale's father, and the spoiled sons are forced to share a room. Comedy, with Will Ferrell, John C Reilly, Mary Steenburgen and Richard Jenkins

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The Contessa is used as bait in a trap to lure an assassin out into the open. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Two sisters receive unwanted gifts at Christmas - severed human ears. Holmes is enlisted to discover where they came from - and uncovers a disturbing tale of deceit and infidelity. Jeremy Brett stars in his curtain call as the famous detective

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar tries to stop a crime ring forming a deadly alliance with psychology students who plan murders. Drama, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A sergeant is injured in a shooting incident and names his informant as the person responsible. However, the suspected man's girlfriend insists that the officer is corrupt and steals away to be with her lover, who has gone into hiding. Lynda La Plante guest stars

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents a preview of day three of Cheltenham Festival, including the Stayers' Hurdle and the Ryanair Chase

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: The Daleys move into the high-spending world of corporate hospitality and find themselves caught between glamour and danger. Samantha Womack guest stars, alongside George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The master baker who turns out a traditional regional delicacy with a Greek twist and the craftsmen who turn raw hides into handcrafted saddles

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey are joined by former Olympic 400m hurdle champion Sally Gunnell and reality star-turned-photographer Anna 'AK' Kelle on the competitive angling show, heading to Essex to try their hands at pole fishing

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar rescues a Russian damsel in distress and agrees to help her fend off art thieves eager to get their hands on her da Vinci masterpieces. Cult adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A security guard is murdered during a payroll snatch, and Regan and Carter are called in to support local investigating officers. However, bad blood between the maverick detective inspector and the force's chief superintendent going back several years manifests itself in an old allegation of corruption being rekindled. Richard Griffiths features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman in the final episode of the crime drama

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: A murder witness is placed under Steed's protection and Tara is the only one who knows where he has hidden her - and is kidnapped by the culprits and held at a sinister hospital

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's brother-in-law Brian suffers a bout of depression, affecting everyone on the manor, and leading the dodgy dealer to make some dubious deals including giving one punter a £500 discount. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at a company manufacturing the hand-sewn Union Jacks seen flying over Buckingham Palace, and a Devon forge that produces wood burning stoves. Plus, an insight into the creation of handmade marbles and the secret recipe of a British kitchen cupboard staple dating back almost 200 years. Ricky Tomlinson narrates

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of family and friends from Kent answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as one-time Coronation Street actor Jimi Mistry (Khalid Nazir), former Emmerdale star Gemma Atkinson (Carly Hope), Loose Women's Nadia Sawalha and veteran actor James Cosmo pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for the charity of their choice. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: A look at the underwater predators the crews and skippers encounter, revealing how they trap and destroy their prey, and play havoc with catch rates

programme.title: Step Brothers (2008)
programme.desc: Brennan and Dale are two 40-year-old men who have never flown the nest. All that changes when Brennan's mother marries Dale's father, and the spoiled sons are forced to share a room. They take an instant dislike to each other, but when their long-suffering parents finally insist they find jobs, they learn to work together. Comedy, with Will Ferrell, John C Reilly, Mary Steenburgen and Richard Jenkins

programme.title: Secrets of the Mega Landfill
programme.desc: The world is running out of space when it comes to room for rubbish - and nowhere is this more true than the US, where thousands of landfill sites are reaching capacity. This documentary visits Orange County in California, home to the ninth largest site in the US - part of an elite group that receive a million tons of waste every year. As many as 750 trucks queue up every day to deposit thousands of tons, but it is not as simple as dumping it all in the ground. At the site is an army of people using innovative technology to deal with the items before they are deposited

programme.title: Great Scientists

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras capture a route through Spain, beginning near Leon, part of the famous north Spanish Pilgrimage, in the Picos de Europa mountains. The railway then runs through the Basque country before the famous Guggenheim Museum greets travellers in Bilbao. In Galicia, the gorges, coastline and sea bridges are a sight for sore eyes, and a speciality cider tour refreshes the train's guests. The journey ends in Ferrol, a spectacular harbour that has been a major shipbuilding centre for most of its history and has been a key city in many wars. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officer Phil Broughton is hot on the heels of dangerous driver heading towards Nottingham city centre, while Lisa and Lewis deal with reports of gun shots heard down by the canal. Elsewhere, an altercation at a petrol station results in a woman assaulting officer Mark Haywood and taking a swipe at one of the camera operators

programme.title: Rose: Making a Monster
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Rose West's childhood, looking back at experiences in her younger years that lead to her becoming one of the most evil serial killers in British history. Throughout the programme, people who knew Rose personally - from childhood neighbours to the solicitor who represented her at the final trial - share stories from her troubled youth, and discuss how this may have impacted her in later life

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: Documentary examining the murder of Janet Scott, who was killed by her partner, Simon Mellors, a man who had been hiding a dark and violent secret - he had spent the last 14 years in prison for the murder of his ex-partner. In the months leading up to her death, Simon showered Janet with gifts and attention, but their relationship drastically changed when Simon became increasingly controlling of who she saw, her home and her life. Feeling suffocated, Janet decided to end things, with fatal consequences

programme.title: Manchester's Killer Nurse: The Stepping Hill Poisonings
programme.desc: Documentary looking back at the complex investigation launched by Greater Manchester Police into a series of suspicious deaths at Stepping Hill Hospital in Stockport in 2011, which saw three people lose their lives and a number of other patients become seriously ill after saline used to treat patients had been tampered with

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera

programme.title: The Highland Vet
programme.desc: When a three-week-old Aberdeen Angus calf arrives in the large animal wing of the practice, Caithness-born vet Shondie faces something she hasn't encountered in over a decade of veterinary training and practice - the calf has been aggressively licked by his mum, causing serious open wounds that require surgery. Away from the practice, 10-month-old Gandalf, a rare breed Zwartbles sheep, is losing weight at an alarming rate, so vet David is called out to investigate

programme.title: This Week on the Farm
programme.desc: There are real fears for the health of Rob and Dave's prize pedigree bull, Jeremy, and the brothers turn foster parents to a trio of alpacas from a fellow farmer struggling in the Covid crisis. On a lighter note, Rob puts Dave through his paces in a contest with the country's fittest farmer, and JB Gill is at sea off the coast of Norfolk as he joins a fisherwoman trying to get whelks back onto Britain's dinner plates

programme.title: 10 Years Younger in 10 Days
programme.desc: Cherry Healey hosts the show in which an expert team transform people's looks using only non-invasive cosmetic techniques. First up are community police officer Suzanne, whose confidence disappears once she takes off her uniform, and Tracey, who has lots of time to herself now that her children have left home. But when she looks in the mirror, she doesn't like what she sees

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War with the Law
programme.desc: Security guards in West Orchards shopping centre in Coventry try to tackle the issue of serial shoplifters, who are behind around 70 per cent of all the thefts in the centre. At Weston Favell Shopping Centre in Northampton, the security team have their eye on a duo suspected of repeatedly shoplifting using young children as decoys

programme.desc: PCs Ben, Launa and Rafal were called to a house in Islington, but instead of the simple case of neighbours arguing that they expected, they found a knife-wielding man intent on wreaking havoc. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica mistakenly believes Joey is infatuated with Phoebe, and Chandler discovers the pleasures of a scented bath. Rachel vows not to find out the sex of her unborn child

programme.title: The Dog Rescuers with Alan Davies
programme.desc: Keira Benham fights to save five malnourished and flea-ridden crossbreeds. Meanwhile, an abandoned Staffie is given a second chance, a Jack Russell is re-homed, and Joe White finds a young spaniel with a horrific neck wound

programme.title: Great Scientists
programme.desc: Allan Chapman presents a profile of the 20th century's most celebrated scientist, Albert Einstein. His ideas were central to the development of many of today's technologies, including space travel, mobile phones, digital television and the atomic bomb

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice and presenter Alistair Appleton offer advice to the owner of a loft apartment in Manchester. Although it is situated in a highly desirable area, the property is full of clutter and no one is prepared to offer the £115,000 asking price

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice and Alistair Appleton look back over home improvement highlights from the series, and focus on making the most of original features

programme.title: A Man Alone (1955)
programme.desc: A gunman stumbles across the bodies of passengers murdered in a stagecoach robbery. Riding to the nearest town to report the crime, he finds himself accused of the killings and faces a battle to prove his innocence. Western, directed by and starring Ray Milland, with Raymond Burr, Lee Van Cleef, Mary Murphy and Ward Bond

programme.title: Detective Story (1951)
programme.desc: A moralistic New York cop is determined to ensure the conviction of an illegal abortionist implicated in the deaths of several women. However, his obsession leads him to lose control, and he is disturbed to discover his own wife was once one of the doctor's patients - a secret she had kept from him. Police drama, starring Kirk Douglas, Eleanor Parker and William Bendix

programme.title: Thunder Bay (1953)
programme.desc: A former Navy engineer comes up with a plan to establish an offshore oil drilling site on the Louisiana coast. However, his scheme is threatened by dangerous weather conditions, his own treacherous financial backers, and irate locals who fear for their fishing business. Action adventure, with James Stewart and Joanne Dru

programme.title: At Gunpoint (1955)
programme.desc: A peaceable shopkeeper becomes a local hero when he accidentally kills the leader of a gang of bank robbers. However, when the dead bandit's partners in crime come looking for retribution, he finds the friends and neighbours who praised his bravery are now about to abandon him. Western, starring Fred MacMurray, Dorothy Malone and Walter Brennan

programme.title: Masters of the Universe (1987)
programme.desc: The heroic warrior He-Man sets out to bring an end to the evil Skeletor's reign of terror, but is transported to Earth by a cosmic key, with his arch enemy's henchman in hot pursuit. Fantasy adventure, starring Dolph Lundgren, Frank Langella, Meg Foster and Billy Barty

programme.title: Film4 Interview Programmes
programme.desc: Stars and directors talk about the experience of making movies in this compendium of personal and practical reflections

programme.title: Night School (2018)
programme.desc: Seventeen years after dropping out of high-school group because he couldn't concentrate during a test, barbecue grill salesman Teddy Walker finds himself unemployed following an accident. In the hope of getting a job at a financial investment firm, he and a band of troublesome adults are forced to attend night classes. Comedy, starring Kevin Hart and Tiffany Haddish

programme.title: 47 Meters Down (2017)
programme.desc: Two sisters vacationing in Mexico are trapped in a shark cage at the bottom of the ocean. With less than an hour of oxygen left and great white sharks circling nearby, they must fight to survive. Horror, starring Mandy Moore, Claire Holt and Matthew Modine

programme.title: Nebraska (2013)
programme.desc: An elderly alcoholic is mistakenly convinced he has won a sweepstakes prize, and keeps trying to travel from his Montana home to Nebraska to collect it. His son ultimately agrees to drive him there despite knowing it is pointless, but the journey ends abruptly when they become stranded in the town where the old man was born. Drama, starring Bruce Dern and Will Forte

programme.title: Three Violent People (1956)
programme.desc: A tough rancher falls for a woman he encounters at the end of the American Civil War, and promptly sweeps her off her feet. However, the couple's attempts to settle into married life are threatened by dark secrets in her past, as well as rising tensions both within the family, and on the political scene. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Anne Baxter, Gilbert Roland and Tom Tryon

programme.title: Ride Lonesome (1959)
programme.desc: A bounty hunter captures young killer Billy John, but his real motive is not the cash reward - he has an old score to settle with his prisoner's brother Frank. As the journey to bring the murderer to justice gets under way, he is counting on a showdown with Frank and accepts the help of two outlaw gunmen for added protection, aware they will try to turn Billy John in themselves for the reward and a pardon. Western, with Randolph Scott, Lee Van Cleef, James Best, James Coburn and Pernell Roberts.

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Ford's Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones

programme.title: Carry On Sergeant (1958)
programme.desc: A military sergeant approaching retirement hopes to end his career on a high note by seeing his last lot of recruits come first in an annual competition to find the best squad in the forces. However, he is not prepared for the hopeless band of misfits placed under his command. The first in the comedy series, starring William Hartnell and Bob Monkhouse

programme.title: Sahara (2005)
programme.desc: An explorer stumbles upon evidence that an American Civil War battleship, which was laden with treasure when it sank without trace, has ended up somewhere in the Sahara. However, he is deflected from his mercenary quest to find the hoard by an encounter with a tenacious doctor, whose battle to stop the spread of a deadly disease could be linked to his own mission. Adventure, starring Matthew McConaughey, Penelope Cruz and Steve Zahn

programme.title: Misery (1990)
programme.desc: A successful author finds his relief at being rescued from a near-fatal car crash is severely tempered by the discovery that his saviour is a fan with psychopathic tendencies. The situation takes a turn for the worse when she discovers he has killed off her favourite character - and sets about coercing him to do a rewrite in nightmarish fashion. Oscar-winning thriller, starring James Caan, Kathy Bates and Lauren Bacall

programme.title: Girls Trip (2017)
programme.desc: A lifestyle guru is offered the chance to be a keynote speaker at a music festival in New Orleans. She decides to invite three old friends she has drifted apart from, hoping to turn a working weekend into a wild vacation and a chance to rebuild their bonds. After a confrontation gets them kicked out of their hotel, they hit the streets of the Big Easy and reconnect with their wild sides. Comedy, starring Regina Hall, Queen Latifah, Tiffany Haddish and Jada Pinkett Smith

programme.title: Wild Tales (2014)
programme.desc: Anthology of six stories in which ordinary people react to everyday social grievances in unexpected ways, leading to bizarre consequences. The tales include a chance encounter on a plane, a tragic revelation at a wedding and an incident of road rage that turns into class warfare. Comedy drama anthology, starring Dario Grandinetti and Maria Marull. In Spanish

programme.title: Tora! Tora! Tora! (1970)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama recounting the bombing of Pearl Harbour from both the Japanese and American perspectives, reconstructing the events leading up to the devastating attack on the US naval base in 1941. Starring Martin Balsam, So Yamamura, Joseph Cotten, James Whitmore and Jason Robards

programme.title: Rawhide (1951)
programme.desc: Outlaws hold staff and a passenger and her baby hostage at a remote stagecoach station while awaiting the arrival of a shipment of $100,000 in gold bullion. The nervous captives try to stay calm, but the tension between the gang leader and a jumpy henchman grows fraught, ending in a frantic shoot-out - with the infant in the thick of the fray. Western, starring Tyrone Power, Susan Hayward and Jack Elam

programme.title: Hatari! (1962)
programme.desc: A group of reckless animal trappers travels through the wilds of Africa, but the arrival of a beautiful photographer looks set to cause upheaval among the group. Comedy adventure, starring John Wayne, Red Buttons, Hardy Kruger, Elsa Martinelli and Michele Girardon

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Out of the Shadows (2016)
programme.desc: The sewer-dwelling heroes discover their arch enemy Shredder has returned from another dimension, and made a deal with an alien warlord. Assisted by two mutant henchman, Shredder goes in search of a lost device that will allow his new ally to mount an invasion of Earth. Action adventure sequel, starring Megan Fox, Will Arnett and Stephen Amell

programme.title: The Great Wall (2016)
programme.desc: In the 11th century, a group of Europeans set out for China to discover how to make gunpowder. However, on arrival their claims to be on a trading mission are disbelieved and they are arrested and imprisoned by the Nameless Order within the Great Wall - just as it is about to be beset by giant, lizard-like monsters. As wave after wave of attacks and surprise tactics batter the Wall, Europeans and Chinese battle hoards of the alien creatures in a series of escalating conflicts leading to a final confrontation with the alien queen. Fantasy adventure, starring Matt Damon, Willem Dafoe and Tian Jing

programme.title: Nobody's Fool (2018)
programme.desc: The recently paroled Tanya suspects her high-flying sister Danica is in an online relationship with a man who may not be what he seems. Eventually Danica, her friend Kalli and Tanya attempt to get to the bottom of the mystery. Writer/director Tyler Perry's romantic comedy, starring Tiffany Haddish, Tika Sumpter and Whoopi Goldberg

programme.title: Dance with a Stranger (1985)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about former prostitute Ruth Ellis, who embarks on a passionate affair with a racing driver. When he announces he's to marry someone else, she is driven to despair and resorts to murderous measures. But her actions make her the last woman to be hanged in Britain. Drama, starring Miranda Richardson, Rupert Everett, Stratford Johns and Ian Holm

programme.title: Black-ish

programme.title: Black-ish

However, things go wrong when Tucker accidentally launders the lucky jersey. Comedy, starring Derek Theler

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's friends all fall for his sexy twin sister, who is paying him a visit, but he declares he will have the final say on who can date her - an announcement that particularly dismays Leonard. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny finds out Leonard's family never celebrated his birthdays, so she enlists Sheldon's help to organise a surprise party, while Howard gets him out of the way by staging an emergency hospital visit. Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki star

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Meanwhile, Amy and Bernadette try to persuade Penny to go on a date. Comedy, guest starring LeVar Burton, with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Mayim Bialik

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Meanwhile, chaos ensues at the precinct after a computer virus publishes everyone's internet browser history

Can Boyle save the day?

programme.desc: Elated at being named one of People magazine's '30 to Watch Under 30', Raj makes a pass at Penny and becomes very arrogant, making it hard for Leonard to maintain a positive perspective on his friend's new-found confidence. Guest starring Charlie Sheen

programme.desc: Howard receives shocking news as everyone prepares for the re-opening of the comic-book store. Penny tries to teach Sheldon how to 'let it go', but she is unable to follow her own advice when she learns something infuriating about Amy. Comedy, guest starring Nathan Fillion, with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Rainbow has a meeting with one of Kyra's teachers and finds out she has an aptitude for chemistry. Meanwhile, Andre Jr shakes things up at Stevens & Lido

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp plans to surprise her guests with two main courses - roast beef with mulled wine gravy, and masala cauliflower with coconut stuffing for the vegetarians

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps teenage sweethearts Terry and Kay find a property in the Costa Almeria area of southern Spain on a budget of £100,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit landed gent Sir Benjamin Slade as they search for more upcycling prospects. Simon converts a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround, while Henry refurbishes a club fender fire surround and turns a quick profit on a Fordson tractor

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners. Henry and Simon realise profit potential in an old crop sprayer, a cast iron fire guard and some old wooden workshop clamps

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The temperatures soar as Dick and Angel face the busiest period of the year, as the school holidays coincide with peak wedding season. Dick comes up with an ingenious solution to stop wedding cakes from melting in the heat, but it involves piercing the chateau's walls. The moat is transformed into a boating lake, giving visitors a chance to cool down, and the family takes to the water in a home-made punt

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: In Hertfordshire, Charlie Luxton helps Roz and Mick Abrahams build a compact contemporary home at the bottom of their garden. Mick and Roz are downsizing from a huge house, and designing a home half the size causes numerous headaches. Charlie shares some bright ideas to demonstrate that, even with a smaller home, it is still possible to think big

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Ship Inn in Dymchurch, Kent, where James Tindall and Jessica Walton hope to impress their guests and be named best value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Thompson Hall Retreat in Norfolk, boasting stylish shepherd huts, and run by mother and daughter duo Amanda Benton and Becci Howlett

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The White House in Swansea, where guests James and Jessica have an unusual toilet encounter in their room

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Jacobs Plough in Bicester, Oxfordshire, run by manager Duncan Froomes and bar supervisor Ryan Hunt who like to mix banter with business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. The Ship Inn is up first, with managers James Tindall and Jessica Walton wanting to let off steam about the surprise comments that made their visit a heated affair

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo head to Herefordshire to pick through a 70-year-old collection, and find a crate of vintage motor oil bottles that could be worth a small fortune

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Two of the young Asian elephants contract the herpes virus, leaving the keepers doing everything they can to save the animals' lives, using anti-viral medication and a pioneering blood transfusion. When silvery gibbon Tilu gives birth, dad Alven has to keep their older son, tearaway Eko, in check. The keepers wonder why jaguars Napo and Goshi have not mated after six years together, and a new species of cricket arrives at the insect house

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The wedding season draws to close, but Dick and Angel need to look ahead to the winter. Dick ploughs up the wild flower meadow, a change that Angel does not entirely approve of, and the couple transform the dilapidated coach house into a cafe, complete with a striking neon sign

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud catches up with Mark and Debbie Sampson in the Lot region of France, to discover how their project of creating a house partly built of straw bales turned out. The couple wanted to use alternative green technology for all the construction work, but could barely communicate with their architect and struggled to integrate with the local community

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: A look back at the transformation of Dick and Angel's 45-room chateau in France from abandoned ruin to sumptuous family home. The series begins by recalling the technical and engineering challenges they had to overcome to make it habitable for their young family. The couple remember their first winter together, when Dick's ingenious solutions were put to the test

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Documentary following West Midlands Ambulance Service at the peak of the Covid-19 pandemic in April 2020, from the perspective of the crews, control room staff and managers as they deal with the human cost of the biggest ever challenge to the service. To get more ambulances onto the frontline, managers deploy student paramedics, with 21-year-old Sam and 20-year-old Caitlyn encountering patients with breathing difficulties, in care homes, and with mental health issues that appear isolation-related

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Zaki, 63, recently underwent a tonsillectomy following a diagnosis of throat cancer, but he has come to A&E after suffering a serious bleed. Doctors try to save 10-month-old baby Amy, who stopped breathing at home, while 21-year-old expectant father Craig appears to have had a miraculous escape after a motocross accident, but has he learnt his lesson?

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the hybrid panel quiz show, putting a comedy spin on Channel 4's long-running words-and-numbers game. Guest team captain David Walliams joins forces with Jonathan Ross, while Jon Richardson teams up with Joe Lycett. Isy Suttie joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, and Rachel Riley takes care of the numbers and letters

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

programme.desc: Kate Quilton uncovers something surprising about small hen's eggs, Matt Tebbutt tracks down where the bubbles in beer come from, and Jimmy Doherty investigates why supermarket burgers should not be eaten when the meat is still pink

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Henry Cole visit a motorbike fan, making table lamps from engines and turning a pallet and iron wheels into a stylish modern coffee table

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The wedding season draws to close, but Dick and Angel need to look ahead to the winter. Dick ploughs up the wild flower meadow, a change that Angel does not entirely approve of, and the couple transform the dilapidated coach house into a cafe, complete with a striking neon sign

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: In West Sussex, Charlie Luxton helps Cathryn and Adam Raybould to build their dream home on Cathryn's family farm. The couple like period houses and hope to give their new build a traditional look. The project has a bumpy start and the whole family must muck in if they're going to finish the job

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at stately home Anstey Hall in Cambridge, with father and son team John and Johnny de Bruyne. The fun starts with a character-revealing afternoon of goose-herding, before the participants dress to impress at a formal dinner. John recounts stories of royal connections - which fails to impress hotel managers Mark and Layla. In the morning, as they tuck in to a self-service breakfast, guest Tim decides that DIY doesn't work at Anstey Hall, but when he notes this on his feedback form, John and Johnny accuse him of game-playing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to clothes-free Clover Spa and Hotel in Birmingham, where owner Tim Higgs hopes to convert his rivals to the joys of naturism. Judging with an extra critical eye are John and Jonny De Bruyne, who received negative feedback from Tim, and a thorough room inspection reveals a dusty porn DVD. Tim tries to encourage his nervous guests to take part in a naked spa experience, but John opts to watch television instead. When feedback is invited, Tim is delighted to discover Mark and Layla would stay again but shocked by the 'porn film' discovery

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Angel Hotel in Coleford, Gloucestershire for the third day of the competition, where a treasure hunt organised by hosts Mark and Layla brings out their guests' competitive sides. Meanwhile, there is confusion at breakfast and Layla's manners are criticised

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Higher Gitcombe in Totnes, Devon, where owners Paul and Lynnie Jolly set out to spoil their guests. Everyone is overwhelmed by the added extras, but naturist Tim is put off by cushions and clutter. After a masterclass in Nordic walking, Lynnie serves up a veg smoothie, which unhealthy eaters Mark and Layla dubiously down in one. Over dinner, Tim challenges his host's organic 'niche'. And Layla, still smarting from his negative feedback, labels him 'weird, naked Tim'. At breakfast, guests are wary as Tim's order goes wrong and Jonny and Layla engage in an eating competition. When feedback is invited, the Jollys are affronted by accusations of special treatment and vow to put the record straight

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition, the owners of Anstey Hall, the Clover Spa and Hotel, the Angel Hotel and Higher Gitcombe meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Having aired their issues, the winners are announced

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to the Devon countryside to meet Derek and Daphne, where Henry finds a very rare tractor, and Simon has his eye on a Raleigh shopper bike

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Special edition of the documentary which gets up close and personal with the animals in Chester Zoo's aquatic habitat. Rare blind cave fish Tiny Tim needs a love potion to help him become a father, and Popeye the chalk bass - the toughest fighter on the reef - needs an urgent operation after injuring his eye in an altercation. The zookeepers hope to breed from Ophelia the Mexican lake salamander, of which there are only 100 females left in the world, while young penguin Frazzle needs to find a mate and settle down to family life. But he cannot seem to stop flirting with every female in sight

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, Dick and Angel harvest their pumpkins in the walled garden and organise a family wheelbarrow race. The basement is the latest room to be transformed, serving as a nerve centre for the whole chateau, a space for storing deliveries, and Dick's workshop. The old piggery is also renovated, in anticipation of some new arrivals

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Andrew Tate and Deborah Mills, who participated in one of the programme's first projects. The pair tried to build a contemporary family home in Amersham, Buckinghamshire, and transform an adjacent water tower into a seven-storey bedroom wing. But while the modern building only took four months to complete, the tower conversion proved more difficult

programme.title: The Great British Dig: History in Your Back Garden
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and a team of archaeologists and historians descend on a suburban road in Kent where they believe a Roman settlement is hidden beneath the back gardens. With just one week to discover the history that may lie below the terraced houses of Florence Road, Maidstone, Hugh goes door-to-door to recruit willing locals to help Richard Taylor, Chloe Duckworth and Simon Elliott dig up their gardens in the search for remains

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges take on Jon Richardson and Victoria Coren Mitchell in the words-and-numbers quiz. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.desc: Desperate Daniels and Leading Lights go head to head as they face tough questions on topics including science, sport and history. Hosted by Jon Snow

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a man who is in cardiac arrest. They fight to keep him alive, while next door his wife hopes for a miracle on their wedding anniversary

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jacqui Lockhart has worked hard all her life, and now that she has the savings available she wants to fulfil her lifelong dream of owning a holiday home on Spain's southern Costa Blanca. With a cash budget of £50,000 and the support of her daughter Judy, the pressure is on for Danni Menzies to help Jacqui secure her dream property

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien turns tank parts into garden furniture, while Henry Cole takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien converts a lantern into a stylish table and Henry Cole changes a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp as the pair visit a private railway

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
