The basement is the latest room to be transformed, serving as a nerve centre for the whole chateau, a space for storing deliveries, and Dick's workshop. The old piggery is also renovated, in anticipation of some new arrivals

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Alan and Lucy Brown to build an upside-down house in Fife with stunning views of the Forth Bridge. Alan knows exactly what he wants and is determined to realise his vision, but with their budget struggling to keep pace with the build, the biggest danger might be that Alan is enjoying the build so much he has forgotten the purse strings

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Four sets of campsite proprietors battle it out to be named best value, with the competition beginning at Amber's Bell Tent Camping in Mannington, Norfolk, a retro-styled pre-erected campsite where artistic host Amber Wykes challenges her guests to go native with a series of bushcraft trials. Over dinner, guests Derek and Julie's opinions on glamping irritate both Amber, and glampsite owners Nick and Sheila. Then back at the tents, brother-and-sister team Russell and Jane aren't too impressed with the sleeping arrangements. In the morning, the self-catering breakfast leads to complaints aimed squarely at Amber

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second day of the competition is at Park Farm Camping and Caravan Site in Norfolk, where husband and wife team Derek and Julie Nice are proud to show off their budget, family-friendly site. Former marksman Derek treats his guests to a spot of clay pigeon shooting, bringing out his competitive side

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to 500-year-old Layer Marney Tower in Essex for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed into luxury glamping lodges by Nick and Sheila Charrington. The guests have little to complain about with the accommodation, but a revelation over dinner changes how they see things. Plus, a treasure hunt organised by the hosts sends one team on a wild goose chase

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Day four is at the Foxhunter Park in Kent, where brother and sister Jane Stacey and Russell Saunders own a caravan site that includes a bistro and entertainment. The accommodation is luxurious but the large scale of the site does not sit well with the campers - and the evening entertainment of a fire-eating limbo dancer leaves most of them cold

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The campers meet for the final time to find out what they thought one another's stay was worth. Amber is offended by Derek's low scoring, while he is thrown by Nick and Sheila's feedback. Russell and Jane have an axe to grind with Derek whose low scoring of their park is made worse by what they see as a personal attack on their hosting, while Nick and Sheila appear to emerge relatively unscathed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo head to Worcestershire where Max transforms some old air tanks into a sports car, while Simon turns old barrels into a trendy bar bench

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the secret life of the animals of Chester Zoo, one of Britain's most popular zoos, using new micro-rig technology to capture the behaviour of the animals and their close relationships with their keepers. Vets at Chester Zoo treat an elephant calf for the herpes virus, the same virus that resulted in the deaths of two calves the year before. A breeding phenomenon plays out among the extraordinary cardinal fish, and the zoo's humboldt penguins welcome 16 new members to their colony

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge use the handiwork and design skills with which they have renovated their French chateau to give viewers ideas on fixing up their own homes. In the first show, Angel helps Miriam transform her 1970s bathroom into a relaxing sanctuary - without changing either the suite or the tiles - and Dick digs out his old handmade smoker to help teach Phil the ancient art of cheese smoking. The couple also advise Tom and Sim how to bring their unique front door into the 21st century

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to the Midlothian countryside to visit Pru and Richard Irvine, who planned to build a family home on an old industrial site. The biggest hurdles they faced were blending the property in with the landscape and working the long hours needed to manage the project. Kevin learns how this scheme fared and whether the locals' fears about an out-of-character building were proven valid

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: The return of the legal drama. Diane finds herself living in an alternate reality where Donald Trump was never elected and Hillary Clinton is the current president of the United States. But while Diane is overjoyed, she soon realises how a different outcome to the 2016 election might have unexpected consequences. Starring Christine Baranski

programme.title: The Tudors
programme.desc: Henry is astounded to discover that his ally Emperor Charles V of Spain has freed France's King Francis I from prison. Another blow comes when he is turned down by Anne Boleyn after offering to make her his only mistress

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Eighty-four-year-old Paula is referred to St George's for urgent treatment, after a routine blood test detected that she has a dangerously high level of potassium in her blood. Meanwhile, 74-year-old Derek is brought into A&E after suffering a suspected stroke. As he goes for emergency surgery, his devoted wife Mary is determined to help him recover. Plus, 31-year-old fireman Glenn arrives with a suspected abscess in his throat; he is joined by his twin brother Dean, and the pair prove to be inseparable

programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Jessica Hynes and guest captain David Walliams take on Jon Richardson and Rhod Gilbert. Sam Simmons joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a culinary journey by sea and land to explore the Far East's most diverse food cultures. He begins in Cambodia, where tourists are beginning to return after the fall of the Khmer Rouge regime. As well as discovering the joys of freshly caught swimmer crabs with green peppercorns, he learns how to cook the country's most famous dish - fish amok - and samples lok lak, made with marinated beef. He also visits the Angkor Wat temple and joins a rice barge to travel down the Mekong River

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian travels to Mongolia, the most sparsely populated country on Earth. With the aid of a local TV host, he spends a week exploring the vast nation. He tries his hand at traditional wrestling and shamanic rituals, encounters an enormous statue of Genghis Khan, takes part in a hunting trip with eagles, and visits an energy centre in the middle of the desert. He also discovers how relationships are formed among nomadic societies, where people often live more than 100 miles away from anyone else

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: The docudrama focuses on the machinations of ambitious aristocrat the Earl of Essex as he tries to take over Robert Cecil's spy network in an attempt to gain control over the ageing Elizabeth I and hold the power to choose the next king of England

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Elizabeth is dead and James VI of Scotland travels south to take the throne at the invitation of Robert Cecil, but the new king has his own ideas and has little time for the late queen's spymaster. Soon Cecil's problems worsen when learns that his arch enemy John Gerard has escaped detention and made contact with a splinter cell in the Catholic underground with a plan to blow-up Parliament. Cecil must negotiate between warring factions to bring down what would become known as the Gunpowder Plot

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Nicholas Day explores the case of Edith Thompson, who conspired with her lover to murder her husband in 1922 - a story that inspired Alfred Hitchcock

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: Divers search a newly discovered wreck as they investigate the story of the SS Justicia, sunk in the First World War by U-Boats off the coast of Ireland

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: A marine biologist and a deep-sea diver explore Chuuk Lagoon, one of the world's greatest underwater graveyards, for a wreck discovered by sonar nearly 20 years ago

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on his final journey in London, exploring Albertopolis in south Kensington and reaching dizzying heights inside a Victorian landmark. At Vauxhall, he learns about the darker side of London's flower market in Bradshaw's day and ends at London Bridge, where two stations are becoming one and a new concourse is being built

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new journey following his Bradshaw's handbook from the heart of the industrial East Midlands to the island of Lindisfarne off the coast of Northumberland. On the first leg, he gives an old engine a fresh start in the railway hub of Derby, heads to Nottingham to find out about the Victorian origins of a well-known high-street chemist and calls at a historic one-time grocer's shop in Grantham, Lincolnshire

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Lyme Park in Cheshire, famously the location for the 1995 TV adaptation of Pride and Prejudice. Items brought in to be valued include a pair of pistols used to protect royal mail coaches from highwaymen, a tea caddy disguised as a pile of books, and a portrait of actress Sarah Bernhardt that once belonged to Elton John

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn goes behind the scenes at London's King's Cross station and finds out about the disused funicular railway hidden in the Avon Gorge in Bristol, blasted out of the cliff

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A two-hour special featuring a deserted Caribbean city, an American ghost town still burning and an Arctic settlement facing a terrifying future

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The story of a bear that fought alongside the Polish army, which became a terrifying modern myth among the German army during the Second World War

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchill's plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Lyme Park in Cheshire, famously the location for the 1995 TV adaptation of Pride and Prejudice. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne, with this leg beginning in Boston, south Lincolnshire, where he explores the connection between the town and its American namesake. At Southwell, Nottinghamshire, he discovers the origins of a favourite Victorian apple before heading into West Yorkshire to visit an imposing institution built in Menston to provide asylum for those with mental health issues. In nearby Wakefield, Michael boards one of Britain's least frequent services, before heading to Hensall, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey in the East Yorkshire town of Hessle, where he learns about the technology that made the Humber Bridge possible. In nearby Hull, Michael hears from local MP Alan Johnson about anti-slavery campaigner William Wilberforce, before travelling to Scarborough Castle, where the founder of the Quaker movement was once imprisoned. His last stop of the day is York, where he learns what made the city a centre for the sweet-making industry

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn explores Huddersfield's Grade I listed station and examines the station's clock, and visits Cologne's magnificent Hohenzollern Bridge in Germany

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Two iconic classics find their way to the Mathewsons' auction room. Dave heads to north Manchester to pick up an Austin A30 and a Morris 8E that's had an alternative paint job

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of admiring Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his immaculate 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews return home from a dreary holiday to find the neighbours about to sell their house to a weirdo - and while Victor is busy moaning about everything under the sun, Margaret considers embarking on an affair. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins the fourth leg of his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne in Middlesbrough, visiting one of the last iron foundries in the town and helping cast a carrot valve for a steam engine. His next stop is Darlington, Co Durham, where he meets the editor of the Northern Echo and finds out about the colourful history of WT Stead, one of his predecessors. Michael then heads to St Paul's monastery in Jarrow, Tyne and Wear, to learn about eighth-century monk the Venerable Bede, known as 'the father of English history', before finishing in Hexham, Northumberland, where he visits a historic ginger beer emporium

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Newcastle on the final leg of his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne, finding out about the world's earliest swing bridge and its inventor William Armstrong. He also travels by boat from the port of Seahouses to examine the story of lighthouse keeper's daughter Grace Darling, who, along with her father, rescued nine people from tumultuous seas in 1838. On the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, Michael explores the lime kilns and finds out how Christianity spread from here across northern England in the 7th century

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits Ribblehead Viaduct in the Yorkshire Dales, talks to the man who saved it from closure and tours the breathtaking stations of the Hungerbergbahn in Innsbruck

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins in the ruinous gardens at Aberglasney House near Llandeilo before riding shotgun in the driver's cab on one of the most scenic routes in Britain. Over the border in Leominster, Herefordshire, he steps out onto the dance floor at the Lion Hotel Ballroom, where a grand ball was held to celebrate the opening of the Ludlow to Hereford railway, and finishes this leg of his journey at a traditional cider house in Hereford

programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new journey following his Bradshaw's handbook from south-west Wales to Cambridge, beginning in Pembroke Dock, where Queen Victoria's royal yachts were built. In the little market town of Naberth he investigates what caused rebels to dress up as women, later spending the night at an inn in Carmarthen where Horatio Nelson once met Emma Hamilton. Next day, the former politician pitches in with the volunteers who look after the Gwili Heritage line and ends his leg in Swansea, where he learns how to pose for a photograph in Victorian style

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins in the ruinous gardens at Aberglasney House near Llandeilo before riding shotgun in the driver's cab on one of the most scenic routes in Britain. Over the border in Leominster, Herefordshire, he steps out onto the dance floor at the Lion Hotel Ballroom, where a grand ball was held to celebrate the opening of the Ludlow to Hereford railway, and finishes this leg of his journey at a traditional cider house in Hereford

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins underground at Big Pit coal mine in Blaenavon, where he discovers how Victorians toiled night and day to power the Industrial Revolution. On the River Usk, he learns about 19th-century developments in angling before heading to Ascott-under-Wychwood in Oxfordshire - once the scene of a farm labourers' dispute that ended in rioting. Michael concludes this leg of his journey at Blenheim Palace

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning in the heart of academia in Oxford, Michael Portillo visits the Bodleian library to see some Victorian treasures, including Mary Shelley's Frankenstein manuscript and a pocket-sized edition of Bradshaw's Companion. At Bicester, he investigates two new rail projects, finds out about Victorian philanthropy in Bedford and finishes in Luton, where he explores the dark arts of the hat-maker

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn follows his family's roots by exploring Metroland, the area around the Metropolitan Railway, and in Great Malvern learns about the woman behind a decorative 1860s station

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Abandoned projects of the space age, including the HARP gun in Barbados and the mountainside Barcroft Observatory in California

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The world's most impressive disused railway bridges, including the biggest wooden trestle bridge in the world, which was closed down due to hurricane damage

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: A Japanese secret society's repeated attempts to kill Charlie Chaplin and Hitler's plan in 1942 to use educated dogs to do a soldier's job

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins underground at Big Pit coal mine in Blaenavon, where he discovers how Victorians toiled night and day to power the Industrial Revolution. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Oakham in Rutland, where he learns about a noble tradition dating back to the Middle Ages and decides to take part. Heading east to Stamford, Lincolnshire, he discovers why the town is such an attractive location for period dramas, while a ghoulish scene awaits in Peterborough when he visits a Victorian operating theatre where railwaymen were treated. Michael's last stop on his journey is Christ's College at Cambridge University, where he finds out about the student days of Charles Darwin

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the country to discover how the railways have affected people and communities, and the legacy they left. The first leg of a journey through north-west England begins in Carlisle, where investigates the Victorian appetite for the custard cream, before braving a perilous descent into the only operational slate mine in England and discovering a miniature railway

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: An impressive collection of five iconic cars arrives at Thornton le Dale, including a 1967 Crayford Corsair and a 1966 Ford Zodiac which emerge from a garage in Selby

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Neuschwanstein Castle in the Alps of southern Germany. Created for eccentric king Ludwig the second in the 19th century, the castle is both a visual and engineering marvel that was an influence on Walt Disney. Containing a huge throne room and man-made cave, it took 17 years to build but was never fully completed after Ludwig was deposed, incarcerated and mysteriously died in 1868

programme.desc: The chef heads to Eagle Rock in California to try to change the fortunes of Capri, a grubby restaurant which often remains closed during lunchtime because of the twin owners' lazy attitude. A crisis ensues as soon as Gordon Ramsay instructs the duo to clean the place, revealing that only tough measures are likely to save the business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bettina and Eamon have spent nearly 30 years in military accommodation moving with Eamon's job. They are now ready to start the next chapter of their lives by buying the first property they will have bought together. They have chosen the Costa Calida in Spain and are hoping one of the five properties Danni Menzies has found within their £100,000 budget prove be perfect for them

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Photographer Nat and budding artist and writer Ruth from Leicester fell in love with Tarn in France on holiday and were so inspired by the region's quaint villages and beautiful scenery that they now want to buy their very own holiday home there. Laura Hamilton lines up five properties for their budget of £90,000, but will any of them be the perfect place?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The host helps Leicester couple James and Drew restore their period house, reclaiming its classic Georgian dimensions and features. In Brixton, south London, a Victorian living room has had all its period features ripped out and replaced with 1970s kitsch and fake Tudor. Can George turn this travesty into a smart and sophisticated family sitting room? Plus, he visits a 300-year-old silk weaver's house in Spitalfields, London, that is perfectly adapted for stylish modern living

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge's to-do list is as long as ever, and with no guests, weddings or events at the chateau this year, they use their engineering and design skills to tackle some major projects. First on the list is transforming one of the old outbuildings into a supersized workshop for chateau-sized jobs

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Oprah with Meghan and Harry and the subsequent news that Piers Morgan had resigned as co-host of Good Morning Britain. Other programmes being considered include Paranormal Lockdown UK, The Celebrity Circle for Stand Up to Cancer, Teen First Dates, Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby and BBC News coverage of NHS pay

programme.title: Two Sisters, One Body
programme.desc: The story of Mexican conjoined twins Carmen and Lupita Andrade, who are omphalopagus - meaning they are attached at the lower abdomen, exploring the challenges they face on a daily basis. At the girls' birth, their parents Norma and Victor faced an impossible dilemma - attempt separation, where only one twin would survive, or stay together. With experts in the United States, the family travelled to Connecticut, where 17 years later they remain on a discretionary visa programme, near the medical facilities that are crucial for their continued quality of life

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Matt Lucas presents as four more famous faces put their baking skills to the test for charity. Hollywood star James McAvoy, singer and Voice coach Anne-Marie, writer and comedian David Baddiel and former athlete Kelly Holmes are this week's quartet, whose challenges involve decorative tarts, a savoury technical and rendering animals in cake form

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets entrepreneur Paul Wilkinson and his wife Amy, who are building a giant new home on a 16-acre plot in Lincolnshire. The superstructure will be comprised of five circular buildings made up of interlocking timber cylinders, linked by raised wooden walkways that hang over a fishing lake. The complicated build proves difficult for Paul's local team, and their ambitious schedule of completing the project in a year soon looks out of reach

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Amateur footage and eyewitness accounts telling the story of the earthquake and subsequent tsunami that caused devastation in Japan in March 2011. The film also discovers how the country dealt with the aftermath of these natural disasters, as people struggled to rebuild their lives and communities

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Wood Green animal charity, who are committed to matching their homeless dogs with prospective owners. An eight-stone Newfoundland makes a favourable first impression on the Coxon family, but staff are worried that his habit of showering everyone with a spray of slobber may take some of the gloss off. Nine-week-old French bulldog Kevin competes with a nervous terrier to win the hearts of loved-up couple Ryan and Katie, while a recently widowed grandmother seeks another 'beating heart' to help fill a home that is feeling very empty

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: Grayson and Philippa Perry are joined by TV presenter Sue Perkins, who crafts an unusual work of art on the subject of food. Grayson also talks to Rose Wylie, who became an art world star in her seventies. There's a peek inside the studio of Lucy Sparrow, who uses felt to create colourful replicas of everyday food items, while comedian Mawaan Rizwan makes his own piece of food-related art. Plus, special guest Nigella Lawson helps choose the favourite public art submissions of the week

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Student Solomon embraces colour in a bid to win the vote of today's model, meditation teacher Julie, while art mentor Diana Ali reminds Tiggy not to lose her head

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Cameras revisit a couple from the Midlands who opened a pasta takeaway on the Costa del Sol. In France, a young family faces an uphill struggle running a glampsite, while a Dorset diver wonders what impact Brexit will have on his Gibraltar business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gary and Janet are ready to take early retirement together in eastern Spain, so Laura Hamilton shows them five properties in Orihuela for their budget of £140,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Sue and Stacey Jones, who have a budget of £72,000, search for a holiday home that will also generate a rental income on Spain's Costa del Sol. Laura Hamilton shows them a selection of properties available for purchase

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Graham Hartridge and Maggie Rogers have been together for 18 months and fancy an adventure. He would love to buy a place in the south-west of France, but she is nervous of taking the plunge and would prefer Devon - much closer to family and friends. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help with their property search

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre enthusiast Jay Coleman hosts the first party in Newport, Gwent, putting on a dramatic evening with an international menu, in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Peace activist Pippa Bartolotti hosts the second of the week's dinner parties in Newport, Gwent, serving up a menu of locally sourced vegetarian food and hoping to entertain her guests by indulging in a spot of fortune-telling. However, a talkative Duncan Powell threatens to dominate proceedings

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Radio journalist Emma Jones hosts the third dinner party in Newport, Gwent, serving up an American-themed menu of crab cakes and pulled pork, and the 23-year-old tries to boost her chances of taking home the prize money with a cheerleading session

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Housewife Sarah Kinnear hosts the fourth dinner party in Newport, Gwent, and her guests are encouraged to attend wearing 'Welsh icon' attire. However, she takes a gamble when she engages in a lively dinner-table debate with Duncan and Pippa

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment boss Duncan Powell hosts the final dinner party in Newport, Gwent, and hopes his Hawaiian-themed evening will impress his guests enough to win the prize. Unfortunately for him, prawn brains and sea bass bones harm his chances of success, so the confident cook tries to rescue the evening with a lemon posset

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits four restaurants he earlier tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or fell by the wayside. First he visits the Spanish Pavillion in New Jersey, then heads to California to see the owners of Kingston Cafe, French-themed La Frite, and Capri

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps house-hunters Taz and Tracy from London, who are looking for a property on Cyprus for £70,000 or less

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul and Debbie from Staffordshire would love to find a holiday home on the beautiful island of Fuerteventura where they can enjoy spending quality time together. They're looking for a two-bedroom apartment near the attractive coastal town of Caleta de Fuste and have a budget of £115,000. Ben Hillman shows them five properties from them to choose between

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: A London tailor hopes to fashion a work studio in his garden - creating a space where he'll be proud to welcome his clients, while in Bristol, an enterprising team are building a cabin in a crane 150 feet off the ground, to raise money for Friends of the Earth. In Japan, George visits a capsule hotel in central Tokyo and a sleep pod measuring only two metres deep and one metre wide

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: Grayson and Philippa Perry are joined by TV presenter Sue Perkins, who crafts an unusual work of art on the subject of food. Grayson also talks to Rose Wylie, who became an art world star in her seventies. There's a peek inside the studio of Lucy Sparrow, who uses felt to create colourful replicas of everyday food items, while comedian Mawaan Rizwan makes his own piece of food-related art. Plus, special guest Nigella Lawson helps choose the favourite public art submissions of the week

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Matt Lucas presents as four more famous faces put their baking skills to the test for charity. Hollywood star James McAvoy, singer and Voice coach Anne-Marie, writer and comedian David Baddiel and former athlete Kelly Holmes are this week's quartet, whose challenges involve decorative tarts, a savoury technical and rendering animals in cake form

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Wood Green animal charity, who are committed to matching their homeless dogs with prospective owners. An eight-stone Newfoundland makes a favourable first impression on the Coxon family, but staff are worried that his habit of showering everyone with a spray of slobber may take some of the gloss off. Nine-week-old French bulldog Kevin competes with a nervous terrier to win the hearts of loved-up couple Ryan and Katie, while a recently widowed grandmother seeks another 'beating heart' to help fill a home that is feeling very empty

programme.title: Caroline Flack: Her Life and Death
programme.desc: Documentary tribute to the TV presenter, who took her own life in February 2020, delving beyond the headlines to reveal the complex woman behind the public persona. The film charts Caroline's career, hears from those closest to her, and explores the pressures that fame, mental, press and social media had on Caroline throughout her life. Contributors include Olly Murs and Dermot O'Leary

programme.title: The Sex Clinic
programme.desc: A man who has had more than a thousand partners and rarely uses condoms comes in for an STI test, and a woman needs a new contraceptive implant fitted inside her arm. A 17-year-old virgin asks for advice ahead of his first time and a young woman wants to know what makes the perfect vagina

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A 23-year-old virgin from Kent picks a partner from six nude candidates - but this proves a nerve-racking experience, since he has never seen a naked woman in his life. Meanwhile, a 27-year-old beauty therapist from London is determined to have a baby before she turns 30, and hopes that one of the men lurking behind the screens will be the perfect father

programme.title: Let's Talk About Sex
programme.desc: Archive footage explores the nation's changing attitude to sex and sex education through the ages, with parents and their children watching, reviewing and reacting to vintage material. Contributors Danny Dyer, Miriam Margolyes, Alastair Campbell, London Hughes and Ulrika Jonsson look at various aspects, from tips on sexual positions to questionable advice on contraception

programme.title: Two Sisters, One Body
programme.desc: The story of Mexican conjoined twins Carmen and Lupita Andrade, who are omphalopagus - meaning they are attached at the lower abdomen, exploring the challenges they face on a daily basis. At the girls' birth, their parents Norma and Victor faced an impossible dilemma - attempt separation, where only one twin would survive, or stay together. With experts in the United States, the family travelled to Connecticut, where 17 years later they remain on a discretionary visa programme, near the medical facilities that are crucial for their continued quality of life

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Brad is keen to get back in art expert Diana's good books after her doubts over his choice of medium. Today's model, James, has a plan that challenges all the artists

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Catching up with the owners of a French B&B where big changes have happened and revisiting a dive centre as its managers enter uncharted waters to attract more customers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bettina and Eamon have spent nearly 30 years in military accommodation moving with Eamon's job. They are now ready to start the next chapter of their lives by buying the first property they will have bought together. They have chosen the Costa Calida in Spain and are hoping one of the five properties Danni Menzies has found within their £100,000 budget prove be perfect for them

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi meets recently divorced Rajula Jasani, who is ready for a fresh start and looks at what is on the market in and around the Spanish coastal town of Nerja. The property buyer plans to invest £100,000 in a second home that she can use with her twin sons and friends, and possibly relocate to in the future

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Diane Rollisson and Robert Somerville want a holiday home, but can't agree on whether it should be in Northumberland or the Midi-Pyrenees. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the couple places in both locations to help them decide

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Simran Samra hosts the first party in east London, and decides to cook meat for her main course, despite being a vegetarian. The fashionista tries to salvage her chances of winning the £1,000 prize by performing a range of comedy accents after fellow competitor Alan Price upsets one of the guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Comedian Yoshki Greenberg hosts the second dinner party in east London, but her decision to surprise the guests by asking them to wear animal-themed pyjama suits does not go down well - leaving her struggling to win them over with a stand-up routine

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Alan Price hosts the third party in east London, and sets out to impress his fellow competitors with a medieval-themed dinner. He hopes to raise the standard of the contest with an ambitious menu, but stumbles at the first hurdle when vegetarian diner Simran Samra panics at the sight of his authentic pig's head as the table centrepiece. Things do not improve as the night goes on, with dirty dinner plates and sloppy hosting leaving the guests less than impressed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bar owner Delia Elliot hosts the fourth dinner party from east London, and she is confident she can teach her young diners the protocol of entertaining guests. However, prankster Alan Price ends up having an argument with her, leading Simran Samra to take evasive action

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Doctor Chintu Gademsetty hosts the final dinner party in east London, and is confident of impressing his guests with a 1980s-themed evening. Even though his dance competition is not a hit with all of the diners, his food goes down a treat, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In this revisit episode, chef Gordon Ramsay goes back to Mojito, the Junction (formerly known as Flamangos) and Bazzini to check in with the owners he helped in the past and find out whether his advice has been put to good use. Will he be pleased with their progress?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Lynne from Yorkshire have devoted their lives to looking after their six children and foster children. Now they're ready to find a holiday home in southern Spain that they can use for extended holidays with all their family. Laura Hamilton pulls out all the stops to show them five properties around Mojácar for their budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After holidaying in Spain for the past decade, London couple Desiree and Pal feel now is the time to buy their own holiday home around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca and have a budget of £65,000 at their disposal. Jasmine Harman helps the pair find their ideal property

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two shows highlighting the way working from home is changing the way many people live. Northampton newlyweds Nikki and Andy both worked from home but their cluttered terrace was close to bursting point. Meanwhile, Amber ran her own fitness classes from her home and wanted somewhere brighter and bigger for her clients. Could Kirstie persuade them to go with her redesigns or would Phil find either couple a much more suitable new place?

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Oprah with Meghan and Harry and the subsequent news that Piers Morgan had resigned as co-host of Good Morning Britain. programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks involving the perils of airport security, fashion for insects and the creative application of cling film and gaffer tape to Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall. programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets entrepreneur Paul Wilkinson and his wife Amy, who are building a giant new home on a 16-acre plot in Lincolnshire. The superstructure will be comprised of five circular buildings made up of interlocking timber cylinders, linked by raised wooden walkways that hang over a fishing lake. The complicated build proves difficult for Paul's local team, and their ambitious schedule of completing the project in a year soon looks out of reach

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby. Plus, an east London landlord who found half a dozen strangers living in his house, after his original tenant had run off and sublet the property. Meanwhile, Harrow Council raid a small two-bedroom flat in unsafe conditions

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Catching up with the victims and villains of the housing rental market. Rachel intended to rent out her Snowdonia cottage for only three months. Four years later her tenant is still there and not paying any rent. Plus, the stories of Stevenage landlord Derek, whose tenant Kellie proved so troublesome that the police had to be called, and Claud from south London, who was left £30,000 in debt when a professional couple split up

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve discover a locked wooden case when they are called to evict tenants from a flat in London's Docklands. Opening it up, they realise they urgently need police back-up - and the property quickly becomes a crime scene. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town FC to collect a debt, while Paul's team arrives at a property in Essex to find that they have to remove a woman and her six children

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team carries out a raid that turns up a stash of drugs, a weapon and a large pile of cash, while Jon Peacock is amazed to encounter a half-naked man covered in blood on the wintry streets of Lincoln, but the real surprise comes when the officer finds out what he has been up to. Suspicious driving leads Jim Woolerton to discover super-strength lager and drugs, and a speeding motorist comes out with a very middle-class excuse after being clocked travelling at 110mph

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan Brigginshaw tracks down a driver who has given him the slip on the side roads of Spalding, while Suzie Ranyard comes to the rescue of a motorist with a rather large passenger. Jim Woolerton utilises some of the unit's most advanced kit to catch out a disqualified driver and officers are called out when a dead body is spotted in one of Lincolnshire's many waterways

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The dog unit embarks on a desperate manhunt amid fears that an armed and dangerous character is roaming the streets of Lincoln, while Craggy closes in on a suspected shoplifter with a bag full of surprises. Officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit also tackle a would-be stunt rider and a boozed-up biker who is raising hell on the A-roads

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner get caught up in a dispute between a landlord and a tenant who refuses to move out, and Brian O'Shaughnessy and Delroy Anglin chase a £17,000 debt. In Essex, a couple owe their neighbour £11,000, and on the south coast, debt collection from a Turkish restaurant gets complicated when there is confusion about ownership

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In east London, High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Phil are involved in a brothel eviction, while in south Wales, Stewart and Elmore chase a debt owed by a working mum. Another case sees Stewart and Victor hunting down a £3,000 debt owed by a Liverpool takeaway for rent arrears

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Susan realises Olivia Bell is back in town, and it seems Ms Bell wants to make amends. Kyle has money problems and needs to find a way to afford the rent

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: John invites Susie for a steamy time, not knowing she is digging for information and snooping through his finances. Ari tries convincing Chloe to give her mum another chance

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, new restrictions have provoked mixed reactions, while an angry parker and his brother turn the air blue in Edinburgh as they try to find a space for Burns Night. A bodybuilder enrages his girlfriend and angry parents by parking in a parent-and-child space, arguing he is too big to squeeze out of his car in a standard bay

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A one-off edition of the documentary in which journalist Nick Wallis reveals how CCTV and technological advances are helping to ensure convictions, focusing on criminals in London involved in robberies, thefts, drug crimes, street brawls and weapons offences. In Soho, police officers monitor the activity of the area's 100,000 nightly visitors from a CCTV control room in an underground bunker, while in Lambeth the air support unit is called in when an armed gang are seen rampaging through the streets

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A patient brought into Hull Royal Infirmary by police claims to have swallowed drugs, but he refuses to co-operate with staff, putting them under further pressure. In resus, a patient's life hangs in the balance as the team assess the extent of a serious brain bleed, which could be life-altering, while a 17-year-old arrives after injuring his ankle at a skate park

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Critical care paramedic Rob and doctor Adam fight to save the life of a boy who has been stabbed in the street. The 16-year-old has been knifed in the neck, has lost a significant amount of blood and is struggling to breathe. Elsewhere, Tom Waters is dispatched to 30-year-old bricklayer Daniel, who has sliced his arm with a power saw. He was cutting timber on a workbench using an angle grinder with a circular blade when he lost control and it sliced through his right hand

programme.title: The Rainmaker (1997)
programme.desc: An idealistic newly qualified lawyer teams up with a disreputable researcher to champion the cause of a leukaemia sufferer whose mother is suing an insurance company for refusing to pay his medical costs. As the case progresses, the young legal eagle finds he has much to learn about the dark side of the judicial process. Francis Ford Coppola's courtroom drama adapted from the novel by John Grisham, starring Matt Damon, Danny DeVito, Jon Voight, Claire Danes and Mickey Rourke

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The buy-to-let documentary series looks back at cases from earlier episodes to find out if matters were resolved or have only got worse. They include a Yorkshire landlord who had to call in eviction specialists to remove tenants from the house he inherited from his grandparents, and a bus driver who managed to win back possession of his home from a nightmare tenant. But just as the eviction was going through, she threatened to call the police

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Updates on the story of property developer Jon Morgan, who called in an eviction specialist when the disused tram station he bought as an investment was taken over by squatters. Plus, a couple who rented out their home while they moved to America - only for their tenant to end up owing them £10,000 in back payments - and how a team from Peterborough Council came across a house with life-threatening electrics and some of the worst damp and mould they had ever seen

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul Bohill and Steve and Ben Pinner face their biggest eviction yet - taking on squatters in a large central London office block. With the help of the police, they try to remove a suspected group of 70 from the building, but with a barricade in place, the team faces a challenging operation ahead. Elsewhere, officers are confronted by an army of family and friends when they attempt to enforce a writ at the property of a debtor, and face a difficult eviction at the north London home of a heavily pregnant tenant and her family, after the council fails to pay the rent to the landlord

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A case of drink-driving leads to a hot pursuit on the city streets and carnage on the country roads, while a routine stop for speeding gives a lone female officer a worrying surprise. As devastating storms batter the UK, the helicopter crew is called out to rescue a woman trapped by deadly rising river water

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sgt Jon Moon and his police dog Kanto follow a trail of broken glass and spilt alcohol, leading to a confrontation with a robber wielding a nasty-looking knife. Elsewhere, a fight in a club ends with one man in handcuffs and another in hospital, Barry Peach plays catch-up as a teenage driver tears up the streets of Skegness, and Jon Peacock detects a whiff of cannabis in a quiet rural village

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers step in to deal with a rowdy reveller when things get out of hand on New Year's Eve in Lincoln city centre, while Suzie Ranyard catches out a motorist who claims he has been driving with a dodgy number plate for 18 years. Jon Moon arrests a shoplifter more than happy to admit to his crime and a young lad discovers how hard it is to pull the wool over the eyes of Jon Peacock

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul Bohill and Phil Short must deal with a negligent tenant who fled upon their arrival, forcing the agents to set a trap in order to evict him. Brian O'Shaughnessy and Kevin Stokes chase a debt of £5,000 in Kent, before heading to Surrey to recover £11,000 owed by the owner a bespoke furniture business. Plus, Paul and Steve Pinner's patience is tested to the limit when they are sent to carry out an eviction in London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Delroy and Kevin face a web of deceit in an Italian delicatessen in south London as they chase a huge debt amounting to £29,000. Meanwhile, Steve and Paul must evict a tenant in north London who has not paid her rent for over 18 months, before heading to Cambridgeshire to make one of their most emotional evictions to date. Plus, Brian and Delroy track down a pensioner in Surrey who owes £15,000 for a business he bought four years ago

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: Nando's Saucy Secrets
programme.desc: A look at how a small takeaway chicken shop in Ealing, West London, grew into an empire with more than 400 restaurants in the UK alone

programme.title: All You Can Eat: Secrets of the World's Mega Buffet
programme.desc: The JRC Global Buffet in Watford is one of the biggest restaurants in the UK. Covering 27,000 square feet, and serving up to 350 dishes from all over the world, it can feed as many as 3,000 people every day. Cameras reveal how the company can feed people as much as they want for a set price and keep their business booming

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Brian Conville gives a man a dressing-down for dangerous driving on the school run, and Barry Peach's detective skills come to the fore when he apprehends a fugitive who has been using a false identity. Meanwhile, during an initiative to clamp down on rural crime, officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit find themselves pursuing some stolen tractors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon Moon and his dog Kanto are hot on the heels of some burglary suspects, while spot-checks by the unit on the A1 turn up a carrier bag full of cash. Jason Baxter rushes to the scene of an accident where the driver was thrown clear of the car, only for another crash to take place - this time involving a line of vehicles

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Barry Peach outsmarts a disqualified driver, handler Jon Peacock takes his new sniffer dog Jack to carry out a drugs warrant, Carl Warrener makes a jaw-dropping discovery when he pulls a car over in the early hours and Brian Conville gets mobbed when he deals with a reckless learner driver

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The enforcement agents try to mediate a dispute in a takeaway, and deal with rent evaders living in potentially deadly conditions. Plus, a businessman faces financial ruin

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmore are met with lies and aggression when they track down nearly £3,000 owed by a garage mechanic, while Steve and Ben encounter a retired couple who are being forced to leave their home. Stuart and Vic revisit a young debtor who owe £750 for a laptop computer, and in Oxfordshire, Paul and Steve chase £30,000 owed by a world-famous Formula One racing team

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: With Kyle reeling over the Tram, Roxy and Levi feel guilty and plan a send-off. Shane is caught in a love triangle, and unsuccessfully tries to focus on Amy

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun

Meanwhile, Geoff, who has become the very first Mr Whippy of Benidorm, having bought a van and fitted it out with a soft ice cream machine, faces a potentially terminal problem when his generator blows up

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: It is nurse Jade's final shift in Barnsley Hospital's casualty department and auxiliary nurse Mike is determined to give her a good send-off. In paediatrics, registrar Dr Nick Mani is seeing a nine-month-old boy who has a very high temperature, while physiotherapist Jovan helps a 90-year-old woman who banged her head in the hospital restaurant

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Critical care paramedic Pete Edwards and the Midlands Air Ambulance Trauma Team are dispatched to give lifesaving treatment to a 21-year-old motorcyclist who has been thrown off his bike, with his helmet also coming off in the impact. Elsewhere, Rob Davis battles to save the life of an 80-year-old who is in cardiac arrest on his driveway, and the team is called to a man who has collapsed with a suspected heart attack and is showing symptoms of Covid-19

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A 41-year-old patient is escorted off the premises of Hull Royal Infirmary after verbally abusing a receptionist in the waiting room, but is allowed back into the hospital to have his hand treated after calming down. In resus, Dr Jeremy Osman and a full trauma team attempt to save the life of 18-year-old Jack, who has severe injuries after falling off his scooter at high speed

programme.title: Extraordinary People
programme.desc: The story of 11-year-old Arya Permana from Indonesia, who weighs 30 stone - nearly three times that of an average man. His status as the world's heaviest boy has brought fame and considerable financial benefits - but when he becomes house-bound and his health begins to deteriorate, his parents realise they must seek medical help. Can the doctors' tests get to the root cause of the boy's extraordinary weight gain?

However, when they kill a would-be rapist in self-defence, they make a fateful decision to run from the law, sparking a chain of events destined to end in tragedy. Ridley Scott's drama, starring Geena Davis, Susan Sarandon, Harvey Keitel, Michael Madsen and Brad Pitt

programme.desc: The girls attend a roller-skating contest, and investigate when someone seems to be sabotaging the competition

programme.title: Kong: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Animated fantasy adventures

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: A magical musician steals all the pigs from the village when the Baron refuses to pay him for his services

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack and Tashi need a genie's help to defeat a monster, but find that he has been kicked out of his lamp

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Boy Soldiers of WWII
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the truth behind the boys who lied about their age in order to enlist and fight in the Second World War, and their shocking stories from the frontline

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens, which house a diverse collection of plants and are a leading centre for scientific research and conservation

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: Shaw Somers, Lucy Jones and Dr Giovanni Miletto give advice to a 42-year-old woman who has been eliminating entire food groups from her diet in an effort to combat her traumatising fish odour syndrome. They also help a teenager who worries severe eczema will prevent him fulfilling his dream of becoming an aeroplane cabin crew member. Three hard-drinking students from Leeds put hangover cures to the test, and Dr Pixie McKenna investigates the veracity of health claims made on food packaging

programme.title: The Baby and the Battleship (1956)
programme.desc: Two hapless sailors are lumbered with caring for a baby during manoeuvres at sea. Keeping the youngster quiet proves a daunting task, and soon the whole crew is forced to pitch in to make sure the little stowaway stays safe and warm - and well out of the sight of the captain and his officers. British comedy, with John Mills, Richard Attenborough, Michael Hordern, Lionel Jeffries and Bryan Forbes

programme.title: Eight O'Clock Walk (1954)
programme.desc: A taxi driver is the victim of an April Fools' Day prank when he is called out to a derelict area - but when an eight-year-old girl is later murdered at the site, he is wrongly charged with the crime. A lawyer is moved by the defendant's wife to represent him, only to face a prosecution case made by his own father. Crime drama, starring Richard Attenborough and Cathy O'Donnell

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Forest Hill, Hither Green and Cubitt Town. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Sapphire and Steel
programme.desc: Part four of eight. A hallway in the station has become a re-creation of a submarine where three young men died from lack of oxygen

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens, which house a diverse collection of plants and are a leading centre for scientific research and conservation

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Tony Palmer's landmark series examines the effect the death of Beatles manager Brian Epstein had on the Swinging Sixties. Featuring archive footage of the Rolling Stones, Jimi Hendrix and the Who

programme.title: Sapphire and Steel
programme.desc: The classic series starring Joanna Lumley and David McCallum. Agents from another dimension try to safeguard earth from attempts by evil forces to destroy the space-time continuum

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring Fitzrovia for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Tracking Down Maggie ()
programme.desc: With hilariously revealing results, this documentary exposes sides of the former British Prime Minister Mrs Thatcher that more hands-on portraits might never approach

programme.title: Away (2016)
programme.desc: In Blackpool, an unlikely friendship blossoms between a lonely, suicidal widower and a young woman trying to escape an abusive ex-boyfriend. Drama, starring Timothy Spall, Juno Temple and Matt Ryan

programme.title: The Krays - Mad Axeman (2019)
programme.desc: A gangster is broken out of prison by the Kray twins just before Christmas in 1966. A national manhunt is soon underway to find the fugitive, forcing him to lay low in an East London flat with his minder. Fact-based crime drama, starring Diarmaid Murtagh and Morgan Watkins

programme.title: The Conversations (2016)
programme.desc: A disillusioned dentist tries to become a stand-up comic, but finds he is unable to perform on stage due to his unrequited love for an American woman who is about to return to the States, never to be seen again. In desperation he seeks a way to have her permanently erased from his memory. Comedy drama, starring Haider Zafar and Daniella Down

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Abbey Wood, Ladywell. Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The team offers advice to a 19-year-old fitness instructor who claims to have never eaten a vegetable, and a woman whose menopausal hot flushes are ruining her life. Also examined is a 39-year-old photographer whose diet may be causing her spine to fuse. The results of the first Big Food Trial are announced, and Pixie McKenna investigates the reliability of do-it-yourself food-intolerance tests

programme.title: Turn the Key Softly (1953)
programme.desc: Three women are released from prison and set about the difficult task of staying on the straight and narrow. Drama, starring Joan Collins, Yvonne Mitchell and Kathleen Harrison

programme.title: The Captain's Table (1959)
programme.desc: A gruff cargo skipper is given the command of a luxurious liner, but finds it difficult to mind his manners among the upper-class passengers. Comedy, with John Gregson, Donald Sinden, Peggy Cummins and Reginald Beckwith

programme.title: The Orchard
programme.desc: On a small Caribbean island, a Rastafarian and two colleagues are nurturing a plot of land to grow exotic foods despite tough conditions and the devastating effects of hurricanes

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Sudbury and Perivale for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Next Big Thing
programme.desc: International Arts Judge James Nicholls is searching for emerging painters, sculptors, photographers and street artists to help them develop and give them the chance to be the 'Next Big Thing'

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom discovers he would qualify for a lump sum from his pension if he were married, so tries to strike up a deal with Sally. Meanwhile, Amber decides to impress her new boss by taking up cycling and, much to Tom's annoyance, receives tuition from Roy on how to ride a bike. Comedy, starring Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Jane Asher

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Comedy set in a DIY shop where the customer is always wrong. Martin Freeman, Ken Morley and Peter Serafinowicz star

programme.title: Discover London
programme.desc: Explore hidden gems and alternative areas of the capital, learning more about the cultural history of one of the world's greatest cities

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Spoof action movie about the Greater London Council, with Charles Bronson (Robbie Coltrane) as Ken Livingstone facing a battle to the death with evil cyborg Margaret Thatcher. Also starring Adrian Edmondson, Peter Richardson, Dawn French and Jennifer Saunders, with theme music by Kate Bush

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael Caine buys a birthday present for Mrs Huggett. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Dead Ringers (1988)
programme.desc: Identical twin gynaecologists Elliot and Beverly share everything in their life. Elliot, self-assured and experienced, passes his unwitting sexual conquests to shy and sensitive Beverly when he tires of them. However, when Beverly falls in love for the first time with a patient, the affair drives the brothers apart - and both men begin to lose their grip on their sanity. David Cronenberg's psychological thriller, with Jeremy Irons in a dual role alongside Genevieve Bujold

programme.title: Hound (2017)
programme.desc: An alcoholic loner is snapped out of his misery when his faithful dog is murdered by a local gangster. Overcoming his addiction, he transforms his life by mastering martial arts, then puts his skills to use seeking vengeance. Crime thriller, starring Sacharissa Claxton and Neal Ward

programme.title: Elsa Fraulein SS (1977)
programme.desc: A sadistic Nazi prison guard oversees a train full of sex slaves, who are to be transported to the front lines. Asd she adopts brutal measures to keep her charges in submission, the French resistance plot to set the women free. Thriller, starring Malisa Longo and Olivier Mathot

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom discovers he would qualify for a lump sum from his pension if he were married, so tries to strike up a deal with Sally. Meanwhile, Amber decides to impress her new boss by taking up cycling and, much to Tom's annoyance, receives tuition from Roy on how to ride a bike. Comedy, starring Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Jane Asher

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls meet their biggest fan, but are disturbed by the way she insists on copying everything they do

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band plays a concert in Scotland in honour of the Loch Ness monster

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Kong: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Animated fantasy adventures

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack tries to steal a phoenix egg, but it hatches and unleashes a fiery bird on the Warlord's banquet

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The boys find a fish that can foresee the future, but it predicts a terrible disaster will befall the village

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Harry Patch - The Last Tommy
programme.desc: Harry Patch, Britain's longest-surviving First World War soldier, joins with five other veterans in reflecting on their lives after the 1918 Armistice. Patch, who died in 2009 at the age of 111, recalls the horrors of trench warfare and how he became a passionate advocate for peace in his later years

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom discovers he would qualify for a lump sum from his pension if he were married, so tries to strike up a deal with Sally. Meanwhile, Amber decides to impress her new boss by taking up cycling and, much to Tom's annoyance, receives tuition from Roy on how to ride a bike. Comedy, starring Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Jane Asher

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: A cross-border collie suffers a setback when a disc from its spine blows and Noel Fitzpatrick designs a special bolt to substitute it. A four-year-old cat is taken to the surgery after being hit by a car, and it is bad news for the owners when the X-rays reveal its pelvis is badly damaged and the operation may cost thousands of pounds

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Sudbury and Perivale for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: Stories of courage, suffering and survival from servicemen and women between 1942 and 1945, as revealed by a generation of people now in their nineties and hundreds. This instalment includes the story of Fergus Anckorn who was once the youngest member of the Magic Circle and - at 96 - is now the oldest. He was taken prisoner by the Japanese in Singapore and put to work on the Burma Railway. He managed to survive using his magic to entertain his captors, who rewarded him with food that kept him and his fellow prisoners alive

programme.title: Dear Murderer (1947)
programme.desc: A man believes he has committed the perfect crime when he kills his wife's two lovers in a jealous rage. Melodrama, starring Eric Portman, Dennis Price, Jack Warner, Greta Gynt and Hazel Court

programme.title: The Baby and the Battleship (1956)
programme.desc: Two hapless sailors are lumbered with caring for a baby during manoeuvres at sea. Keeping the youngster quiet proves a daunting task, and soon the whole crew is forced to pitch in to make sure the little stowaway stays safe and warm - and well out of the sight of the captain and his officers. British comedy, with John Mills, Richard Attenborough, Michael Hordern, Lionel Jeffries and Bryan Forbes

programme.title: Whistle Down the Wind (1961)
programme.desc: Three children become convinced that a bearded murderer taking refuge in the barn on their Lancashire farm is Jesus Christ, and do their utmost to protect him and conceal his presence from the outside world. Drama, based on the novel by Mary Hayley Bell, starring Alan Bates, Hayley Mills, Bernard Lee and Norman Bird

programme.title: Discover London
programme.desc: Explore hidden gems and alternative areas of the capital, learning more about the cultural history of one of the world's greatest cities

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Sail the Saronic and Ionian Greek islands with Peter Maneas as he explores their rich history, culture, cuisine, and people as well as their hidden secrets and surprises

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: A drunk John Hurt arrives in the neighbourhood, while Joe takes advantage of Michael's absence by taking over his house. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael throws a fancy dress party at his house to celebrate the 33rd anniversary of the film Zulu, but the mood turns sour when the Monopoly board comes out

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Returned (2013)
programme.desc: A young doctor goes on the run with her bitten boyfriend and their remaining supplies of a protein used to treat a zombie virus. Sci-fi horror, starring Emily Hampshire, Kris Holden-Ried and Shawn Doyle

programme.title: Relentless (2020)
programme.desc: An ex-convict must stay out of trouble for one more day to secure his freedom - but must choose between his principles and his future when he comes to the aid of a prostitute under threat from a gang. Action thriller, starring Steven Murphy and Tiffany-Ellen Robinson

programme.title: This That and the Other (1970)
programme.desc: Anthology of three comic tales. A fading movie star tries to seduce a producer, a suicidal loner is snapped out of his misery by a hippy, and a taxi driver suffers a blow to the head that leaves him experiencing erotic hallucinations. Comedy, starring Victor Spinetti, Dennis Waterman, Vanda Hudson and John Bird

programme.title: Queens
programme.desc: 16th-century Europe was a hotbed of tense international intrigue. Mary Stuart and Elizabeth I confront one another on matters religious, political and personal

programme.desc: Phil Taylor v Raymond van Barneveld. Action from the semi-final in the 2013 World Championship at Alexandra Palace. Both of these players had been in imperious form throughout the tournament, with Taylor dropping just one set to Dutchman Jerry Hendriks en route to the semi-final, and van Barneveld conceding a set each to Simon Whitlock and Brendan Dolan respectively
